  • Write Wave-D plan: create 2026-04-24-v1.8-agentic-backbone.md from this template, pulling the feature list from §5 of the master roadmap.

docs.title: Self-Review Checklist (use after Task 15)
docs.text:
Spec coverage: Every feature listed under the Wave-C row in 2026-04-24-full-parity-roadmap.md § Wave C has a matching task above. Cross-check:

  • /rewind persistent → Task 1
  • /resume → Task 1
  • /fork, /rename, /sessions, /export → Task 2
  • /map → Task 3
  • /blame, /trace, /self → Task 4
  • /budget set, /badges → Task 5
  • Ctrl+T, Ctrl+O, Esc Esc, Tab, Alt+T, Alt+M → Task 6
  • /mode full → Task 7
  • /handoff → Task 8
  • /effort → Task 9
  • /ultrareview, /review, /tdd-gate → Task 10
  • /config, /theme, /skin → Task 11
  • /vim real → Task 12
  • /red-proof → Task 13
  • /tools, /btw, /pair → Task 14
  • paste-as-image, parity matrix, CHANGELOG, Wave-D plan → Task 15

Test count target: ~80 new RED/GREEN contract tests across ~14 new test files. Suite target: 912 → ~990 green (with the same 2 sandbox-skipped + 11 git-sandbox-deselected hosts).

No signature drift: Every new method on InteractiveSession returns str (the _cmd_<name>_text convention from Wave-B); the slash dispatchers (_cmd_<name>(session, args) -> CommandResult) wrap them with the existing _out.*_renderable calls.

Invariants respected: 1. ✓ TDD-first. 2. ✓ No breaking changes (every new arg keyword-only with default). 3. ✓ Evidence before assertion (each task names symbol + test file). 4. ✓ No new optional deps (image OCR deferred to Wave F). 5. ✓ Graceful degradation on missing preconditions. 6. ✓ Local-first stays local-first. 7. ✓ Plan-mode-default-on preserved. 8. ✓ Mock-LLM parity (no auto-LLM call from new slashes).

docs.title: Execution Handoff
docs.text:
Two execution options (same as Wave B):

1. Subagent-Driven (recommended for parallelism) — Tasks 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 are independent (different files); dispatch them in parallel. Tasks 7–14 mostly touch session.py and must run sequentially. Task 15 is the docs + plan, runs last.

2. Inline Execution — Same TDD rhythm as Wave B; faster iteration but no parallelism.

Which approach? Default to subagent-driven for the parallelizable chunk (Tasks 1–5, ~15 minutes wall-clock with 5 parallel subagents), inline for the sequential session.py chunk (Tasks 6–14), inline for the docs (Task 15). Final code-quality reviewer subagent over the full v1.7.5 surface before tagging.

Stop after Task 15 — present the shipped v1.7.5 release + the Wave-D plan file for user review before Wave D.

docs.title: v1.8 "Agentic Backbone" — Wave-D Detailed Plan
docs.text: Created: 2026-04-24, immediately after Wave-C / v1.7.5 shipped. Status: READY FOR EXECUTION (start when v1.7.5 has been reviewed and the user gives the go-ahead). Predecessor: v1.7.5 "REPL Convergence" — shipped 2026-04-24 with +76 contract tests across 15 new files plus a +28 post-review safety patch, total 581 passed / 2 sandbox-skipped lyra-cli suite green (python3 -m pytest packages/lyra-cli). Master roadmap: 2026-04-24-full-parity-roadmap.md § Wave D. Successor: Wave E — v1.9 "Channels, Backends, Eval".

docs.title: Theme
docs.text:
The heaviest wave: turn every Wave-C surface into a real agent. The slash commands are now load-bearing — /red-proof becomes the gate /tdd-gate actually enforces; /ultrareview fans out to real subagents in worktrees; /btw opens a sibling subagent with isolated context; /pair streams thoughts in real time; Ctrl+F actually kills running subagents. The CLI surface stays exactly where v1.7.5 put it; only the engine behind each slash gets replaced.

This wave is mostly engine + lifecycle + permissions. No new slash commands (the matrix is at REPL parity already). No new themes, no new keybinds. Difficulty per feature is high; the wave is large because the engine has many independent moving parts.

docs.title: Invariants (from master roadmap)
docs.text:
1. TDD-first — every task writes the contract test before the implementation; the contract test imports from the new module before the module exists.
  2. No breaking changes — every Wave-C slash continues to work identically; the engine wires behind the existing dispatchers, never changes their signatures.
  3. Evidence before assertion — every ✓ shipped parity-matrix flip names a code symbol AND a test file path.
  4. Optional deps — lyra[rl], lyra[browser], lyra[codeact], lyra[vector], lyra[mcp] extras for the heavy adapters. Base install footprint must remain unchanged from v1.7.5.
  5. Graceful degradation — every feature degrades to a friendly "feature requires pip install lyra[<extra>]" message when the extra is missing; no ImportError reaches the REPL.
  6. Local-first stays local-first — no Wave-D feature pings the network unless the user already opted in via /web or /mcp connect.
  7. Plan-mode-default-on invariant — the agent loop must refuse to write before the user explicitly enters /mode build.
  8. Worktree-only Edits — every subagent that touches files runs in a git worktree rooted at <repo>/.lyra/worktrees/<sub-id>; no Edits land in the user's main checkout until the user accepts the diff via /handoff or git apply.

docs.title: Cross-wave dependencies
docs.text:
• lyra_core.providers.preflight (Wave B library): finally wired into the agent loop here (Task 14).
  • _TurnSnapshot (Wave C, Task 1) gains a tool_calls: list[ToolCall] field in this wave so subagent fan-out replays cleanly.
  • RingBuffer (Wave C, Task 4) becomes the substrate the /blame//trace slashes use to surface real subagent traces.
  • BudgetCap (Wave C, Task 5) gains live deduction inside the agent loop (Task 13) so /budget set 5 actually halts spending at $5.

docs.title: Tasks (15 ordered units)
docs.text: Each task lists: - Slashes / hooks — the user-visible affordance. - Symbols — the new code unit(s). - Test — the contract-test file path + count. - Depends on — Wave-D tasks that must land first.

docs.title: Task 1: Subagent runtime substrate (SubagentRunner)
docs.text:
• Symbols: lyra_core.subagents.runner.{SubagentRunner, SubagentSpec, SubagentResult, SubagentStatus} — orchestrator that wraps an AgentLoop invocation in a worktree, captures stdio + HIR events, surfaces a typed result.
  • Test: lyra-core/tests/test_subagent_runner_contract.py — 6 tests (happy path, worktree isolation, cancellation, tool-output capture, error propagation, HIR scoping).
  • Depends on: —

docs.title: Task 2: SubagentRegistry (live process table)
docs.text:
• Slashes: /agents + Ctrl+F (kill all) + /spawn upgraded.
  • Symbols: lyra_core.subagents.registry.{SubagentRegistry, spawn, kill, list_running, on_complete} — singleton process table with weak refs so the REPL never leaks.
  • Test: lyra-cli/tests/test_subagent_registry.py — 7 tests (register/spawn/list/kill/on_complete + status-line indicator + Ctrl+F empty case).
  • Depends on: Task 1.

docs.title: Task 3: User-defined subagent presets (~/.lyra/agents/<name>.yaml)
docs.text:
• Symbols: lyra_core.subagents.presets.{load_user_presets, PresetSpec}; built-ins Explore, General, Plan, plus hermes-compat leaf / orchestrator aliases.
  • Test: lyra-cli/tests/test_subagent_presets.py — 6 tests (built-ins listed, user yaml loaded, alias resolution, missing file graceful, malformed file graceful, alias collision).
  • Depends on: Task 1.

docs.title: Task 4: DAG scheduler (fan-out + join)
docs.text:
• Symbols: lyra_core.subagents.scheduler.{TaskGraph, Edge, schedule, run_to_completion} — topological executor with depends_on=[id, ...].
  • Test: lyra-core/tests/test_subagent_scheduler.py — 8 tests (linear chain, fan-out + join, diamond, cycle detection, cancellation cascades, partial-failure policy, deterministic order under shuffle, observability hooks).
  • Depends on: Tasks 1–2.

docs.title: Task 5: Variant runs (variants=N + LLM-judge selector)
docs.text:
• Symbols: lyra_core.subagents.variants.{run_variants, VariantResult, judge_with_llm}; full PRM integration deferred to Wave F.
  • Test: lyra-core/tests/test_subagent_variants.py — 5 tests.
  • Depends on: Tasks 1, 4.

docs.title: Task 6: Permissions stack — destructive-cmd + secrets + injection guards
docs.text:
• Symbols: lyra_core.permissions.{DestructiveCommandDetector, SecretsScanner, InjectionGuard, PermissionPolicy}; mode-based gating (strict | normal | yolo); regex packs + LLM-augmented classifiers; lyra[guards] extra for ML classifiers.
  • Test: lyra-core/tests/test_permissions_stack.py — 9 tests (rm -rf detected, secrets matched on AWS/Stripe/GitHub/GCP/Azure /Twilio, injection token detected, permission_mode honoured, allowlist via /less-permission-prompts).
  • Depends on: Wave-C permission_mode.

docs.title: Task 7: Tool-approval prompt (cached per session)
docs.text:
• Slashes: every high-risk tool call shows [approve|reject|always].
  • Symbols: lyra_cli.interactive.approval.{ApprovalPolicy, ApprovalCache, request}; cache keyed by (tool_name, sha256(args)).
  • Test: lyra-cli/tests/test_tool_approval.py — 6 tests.
  • Depends on: Task 6.

docs.title: Task 8: ExecuteCode real (CodeAct sandbox via pyodide / firejail)
docs.text:
• Symbols: lyra_core.tools.codeact.{ExecuteCode, PyodideRunner, FirejailRunner}; lyra[codeact] extra.
  • Test: lyra-core/tests/test_tool_codeact.py — 6 tests (stdout / stderr / return / timeout / sandbox-escape rejected / extra-missing graceful).
  • Depends on: Tasks 1, 6.

docs.title: Task 9: Browser real (playwright)
docs.text:
• Symbols: lyra_core.tools.browser.{Browser, navigate, screenshot, click, type_text, evaluate}; lyra[browser] extra.
  • Test: lyra-core/tests/test_tool_browser.py — 5 tests (navigate→DOM, screenshot bytes, click no-such-selector graceful, evaluate returns JSON, extra-missing graceful).
  • Depends on: Tasks 1, 6, 7.

docs.title: Task 10: Custom user tools (~/.lyra/tools/<name>.py + @tool)
docs.text:
• Symbols: lyra_core.tools.user.{ToolRegistry, register, invoke, _import_user_tools}; lazy import on first invocation; multiprocessing.Process worker for isolation.
  • Test: lyra-core/tests/test_user_tools.py — 6 tests.
  • Depends on: Tasks 1, 6.

docs.title: Task 11: Hooks lifecycle (SessionStart … TurnRejected)
docs.text:
• Symbols: lyra_core.hooks.{HookSpec, HookRegistry, fire}; events SessionStart, SessionEnd, UserPromptSubmit, PermissionRequest, SubagentStart, SubagentStop, FileChanged (via watchdog), WorktreeCreate, ErrorThrown, TurnRejected.
  • Test: lyra-core/tests/test_hooks_lifecycle.py — 11 tests (one per event + plugin-handler ordering).
  • Depends on: Wave-B PluginRuntime.

docs.title: Task 12: MCP full (client + server + trust banner)
docs.text:
• Slashes: /mcp connect, /mcp disconnect, /mcp list, /mcp serve.
  • Symbols: lyra_cli.mcp.{McpClient, McpServer, TrustBanner, ManifestSigner}; lyra[mcp] extra.
  • Test: lyra-cli/tests/test_slash_mcp.py — 8 tests (client connects to loopback, server accepts client, trust banner shown on first use, signed manifest verified, unsigned manifest rejected, disconnect graceful, list reflects state, extra-missing graceful).
  • Depends on: Tasks 6–7.

docs.title: Task 13: BudgetCap live deduction (Wave-C carry-over)
docs.text:
• Symbols: integrate BudgetCap.enforce into the agent loop; refuse to start a turn when the next-turn estimate would push spend over EXCEEDED; emit BudgetAlertEvent.
  • Test: lyra-core/tests/test_agent_loop_budget.py — 5 tests.
  • Depends on: Wave-C BudgetCap.

docs.title: Task 14: Preflight wiring (Wave-B carry-over)
docs.text:
• Symbols: wire lyra_core.providers.preflight into lyra_cli.providers.openai_compatible.{OpenAICompatibleLLM, AnthropicLLM, GeminiLLM, BedrockLLM, VertexLLM, CopilotLLM}; configurable ~/.lyra/settings.json → preflight: { mode: "warn"|"raise"|"off" }.
  • Test: lyra-cli/tests/test_provider_preflight_wired.py — 8 tests covering on/off/warn modes, stream-token estimation, tool-payload accounting, /compact suggestion banner, override via env.
  • Depends on: Wave-B preflight.

docs.title: Task 15: Live-streaming /pair + parity matrix + CHANGELOG + Wave-E plan
docs.text:
• Slashes: /pair on upgrades to a real subscriber on the RingBuffer event stream; thoughts render incrementally.
  • Symbols: lyra_cli.interactive.pair.{PairStreamer, render}.
  • Test: lyra-cli/tests/test_slash_pair_streaming.py — 4 tests.
  • Docs:
  • Update docs/feature-parity.md: flip every Wave-D cell to ✓ shipped (v1.8); add §5e "v1.8 'Agentic Backbone' delta" with a row-per-feature table.
  • Append to CHANGELOG.md.
  • Write the detailed Wave-E plan (2026-04-24-v1.9-channels-backends-eval.md).

Test target: ~95 new RED/GREEN contract tests.

Success criteria:

  • A user can write ~/.lyra/agents/researcher.yaml, run /spawn researcher: investigate X, and the subagent runs to completion with worktree isolation.
  • A user can /spawn 5 variants in parallel and pick the winner via the LLM judge.
  • A user can install a third-party MCP server and the trust banner appears on first use.
  • lyra run --tool browser_navigate "https://…" opens a headless Chromium and returns the rendered DOM.
  • /budget set 5 halts the agent at $5 of cumulative spend (HIR emits BudgetAlertEvent and the UI surfaces a banner).
  • /red-proof packages/foo/tests/test_bar.py is now the gate the agent loop respects: with tdd_gate_enabled=True, an Edit to packages/foo/bar.py is rejected unless the matching red-proof was recorded in the same turn.

docs.title: Definition of done
docs.text:
• All 15 tasks landed with both RED + GREEN tests committed.
  • Full lyra-cli + lyra-core suite green (python3 -m pytest packages/lyra-cli packages/lyra-core).
  • docs/feature-parity.md §1.4 (subagents) + §1.5 (memory) + §1.6 (permissions) + §1.7 (hooks) all flipped to ✓ shipped (v1.8).
  • CHANGELOG.md v1.8 section authored with the same shape as v1.7.5.
  • 2026-04-24-v1.9-channels-backends-eval.md exists with at least task-level granularity.
  • No new dependencies added to base install — every adapter behind a lyra[<extra>] extra.

docs.title: Out of scope (deferred to later waves)
docs.text:
• Full PRM (process reward model) — Wave F.
  • Full evals harness (BFCL, SWE-bench, MERIT) — Wave E.
  • Multi-channel gateway adapters beyond Telegram (Slack, Discord, WhatsApp) — Wave E.
  • ACP bridge full handshake — Wave E.
  • Skill-Creator v2 four-agent loop — covered here as an enabler; full UX polish deferred to Wave F.
  • OCR / vision-tower routing for paste-as-image — Wave F.

docs.title: Wave E — v1.9 "Channels, Backends, Eval" — Detailed Plan
Wave E — v1.9 "Channels, Backends, Eval" — Detailed Plan
docs.text:
Date: 2026-04-24 Owner: Lyra harness team Status: drafted at Wave-D ship; ready for subagent-driven execution. Predecessor: 2026-04-24-v1.8-agentic-backbone.md (✓ shipped) Master roadmap: 2026-04-24-full-parity-roadmap.md

For agentic workers: REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement each task. Each task below ships RED → GREEN → REFACTOR with a contract test in the same commit as the implementation.

docs.title: 1. Goal
docs.text: Match hermes-agent on every channel adapter and remote terminal backend; build the eval gates that Lyra's TDD-first identity needs; ship the multi-modal pieces (vision, voice) and the wiki / onboarding surfaces. After this wave, Lyra is a strict superset of every reference repo on the channel, backend, and eval axes.

docs.title: 2. Architecture
docs.text:
15 tasks landing as semver-tagged minor release v1.9.0 "Channels, Backends, Eval". Each task is a single PR / commit batch with its own RED → GREEN → REFACTOR cycle.

v1.8.0 (✓ shipped, Wave D) ──┐
                             │
                             ├─► v1.9.0 — Wave E — Channels, Backends, Eval
                             │   (~3 weeks, 15 tasks, ~200 new contract tests)
                             │
                             └─► v2.0   — Wave F — Frontier

docs.title: 3. Invariants
docs.text:
Inherited from the master roadmap §4:

  1. TDD-first. RED → GREEN → REFACTOR per task; contract test in the same commit as the implementation.
  2. No breaking changes. Channel adapters extend lyra_cli.channels.* additively; backends extend harnesses.terminal_backends.* additively; existing public APIs stay stable.
  3. Optional deps. Every channel + backend lands as lyra[<name>] — pip install lyra (no extras) must still launch the REPL.
  4. Graceful degradation. Missing optional dep → FeatureUnavailable with the exact pip install lyra[<extra>] fix command.
  5. Smoke vs unit. Tests that touch live network credentials are @pytest.mark.smoke and gated on LYRA_RUN_SMOKE=1. Paid backends (Modal, Daytona) gate additionally on LYRA_RUN_PAID_SMOKE=1.
  6. Doc surface. Each task appends to CHANGELOG.md v1.9.0 entry, flips the relevant cell in docs/feature-parity.md from NOW / stub → ✓ shipped (v1.9.0), and bumps the Verification snapshot version.
  7. Local-first stays local-first. The local-model path (Ollama / LM Studio / llama.cpp / vLLM / MLX-LM) introduced in Wave B and the local backends introduced here must remain a viable end-to-end path with zero cloud creds.

docs.title: Task 1 — Channel adapter substrate (lyra_cli/channels/base.py)
docs.text:
Theme: the abstract base every channel adapter implements, plus the gateway daemon that fans messages from all configured channels into a single agent-loop scheduler.

Contract: - class ChannelAdapter(Protocol) — start(), stop(), send(thread_id, text), iter_inbound() -> AsyncIterator[Inbound]. - class Inbound (dataclass): channel, thread_id, user_id, text, attachments, received_at. - class Gateway — owns a list of adapters, manages a per-thread agent-loop session, dispatches inbound to the right session, pushes outbound replies back.

RED tests (8): - adapter protocol surface (start/stop/send/iter_inbound) - gateway routes inbound to the right session - gateway tolerates an adapter that raises in iter_inbound - gateway emits channel.inbound HIR event - send returns the message id (string) - multiple adapters multiplex in iter_inbound - stop cleanly drains in-flight messages - Inbound round-trips through JSON

docs.title: Task 2 — Slack adapter (lyra[slack])
docs.text:
Files: lyra_cli/channels/slack.py, tests/test_slack_adapter_contract.py.

Optional dep: slack-sdk.

Behaviour: subscribes to RTM / Socket Mode events; maps each channel + thread to a Inbound; uses chat.postMessage for outbound; honours Sec-Slack-Thread-Ts for threading.

RED tests (6): auth dance, inbound formatting, outbound threading, attachment passthrough, graceful 429, missing-token error path.

docs.title: Task 3 — Discord adapter (lyra[discord])
docs.text: Mirrors Task 2 against discord.py. 6 RED tests.

docs.title: Task 4 — Matrix adapter (lyra[matrix])
docs.text: Mirrors Task 2 against matrix-nio. 6 RED tests.

docs.title: Task 5 — Email + SMS adapters (lyra[email] / lyra[sms])
docs.text:
Bundled because they share the "no real-time, just poll / send" UX.

  • Email: stdlib IMAP + SMTP (aioimaplib for async); supports Gmail, Outlook, Fastmail.
  • SMS: Twilio + Vonage backends behind a SmsBackend Protocol.

5 RED tests each (10 total).

docs.title: Task 6 — Long-tail channel adapters
docs.text: Feishu / Lark, WeCom, Mattermost, BlueBubbles, WhatsApp (Twilio), Signal (signal-cli REST), OpenWebUI, HomeAssistant, QQBot, DingTalk, generic webhooks. Each gets a single contract test (test_<name>_adapter_contract.py) that asserts the adapter implements the protocol + a happy-path send. 11 tests.

docs.title: Task 7 — Modal backend (lyra[modal])
docs.text:
Files: harnesses/terminal_backends/modal.py, tests/test_modal_backend_contract.py.

ModalBackend(image="python:3.12", cpu=2, memory_mb=4096) — runs AgentLoop.run_conversation on Modal; streams stdout/stderr back to the parent. 6 RED tests; smoke gated on LYRA_RUN_PAID_SMOKE=1.

docs.title: Task 8 — SSH backend (lyra[ssh])
docs.text: Mirrors Task 7 against paramiko / asyncssh. 6 RED tests.

docs.title: Task 9 — Daytona + Singularity backends
docs.text: Bundled. Each ships a contract test + a happy-path smoke. 8 tests total.

docs.title: Task 10 — Vision toolkit (lyra[vision])
docs.text:
Files: lyra_core/tools/image_describe.py, lyra_core/tools/image_ocr.py, tests/test_vision_tools_contract.py.

  • image_describe(path) -> dict — calls the active LLM with the paste-as-image attachment.
  • image_ocr(path) -> dict — pytesseract / EasyOCR fallback.
  • Wires into Wave-C paste-as-image: when the user pastes an image, the placeholder [Image #N] resolves through image_describe before the prompt is sent.

8 RED tests.

docs.title: Task 11 — Voice toolkit (lyra[voice])
docs.text:
Files: lyra_cli/voice/{stt.py, tts.py}, tests/test_voice_tools_contract.py.

  • STT via faster-whisper (CPU) or openai-whisper (GPU).
  • TTS via OpenAI audio.speech or pyttsx3 (offline).
  • /voice on|off slash that pipes mic → STT → REPL input and REPL output → TTS.

6 RED tests + 2 smoke tests.

docs.title: Task 12 — Session replay (/replay)
docs.text:
Files: lyra_cli/interactive/replay.py, tests/test_session_replay_contract.py.

Step through a persisted session event-by-event with a diff overlay showing what changed at each step. 6 RED tests.

docs.title: Task 13 — Red-team corpus + safety monitor
docs.text:
Files: tests/red_team/<category>/case_<id>.yaml, lyra_core/safety/monitor.py.

A small starter corpus (≥30 cases across prompt-injection, secrets-exfil, destructive-cmd, jailbreak categories) + a monitor that runs the corpus on lyra eval safety and reports regressions.

8 RED tests (4 corpus loaders, 4 monitor behaviour).

docs.title: Task 14 — Golden eval corpus + drift gate
docs.text:
Files: tests/golden/<id>.yaml, lyra_core/eval/drift.py, lyra_cli/cli/eval.py (extension).

A starter set (~20 cases) + lyra eval drift --baseline v1.8.0 that produces a markdown delta report (regressions, new passes, unchanged). 8 RED tests.

docs.title: Task 15 — /wiki + /team-onboarding + parity matrix +
docs.text:
CHANGELOG + Wave-F plan
  • /wiki real (file-system markdown by default; Notion + Confluence via lyra[wiki-notion] / lyra[wiki-confluence]).
  • /team-onboarding generates a teammate-targeted ONBOARDING.md from skills + recent sessions.
  • docs/feature-parity.md §5f delta table for v1.9.0.
  • CHANGELOG.md v1.9.0 entry.
  • 2026-04-24-v2.0-frontier.md detailed plan written.

docs.title: 5. Test target
docs.text:
• ~200 new RED/GREEN contract tests.
  • Combined pytest run target: 1143 → ~1343 green.
  • Smoke-gated tests: ≥30 (channels), ≥10 (backends), ≥5 (vision / voice). Default suite must run without any LYRA_* env vars.

docs.title: 6. Success criteria
docs.text:
• lyra gateway start --channel slack,discord,email starts the daemon and three channel listeners with no extra wiring.
  • lyra run --backend modal --image python:3.12 --task "…" runs the agent loop on Modal with results streamed back.
  • lyra eval drift --baseline v1.8.0 produces a markdown drift report.
  • lyra voice on enables mic + speakers; lyra voice off restores text-only.
  • The starter red-team corpus runs in <30s and reports zero regressions on the v1.9.0 baseline.

docs.title: 7. Risks & mitigations
docs.text:
• Live channel credentials. Many channel APIs require working tokens for end-to-end smoke. Mitigation: ship FakeBackend doubles for the unit tier; smoke tests gate on per-channel env vars (LYRA_SLACK_TOKEN, LYRA_DISCORD_TOKEN, …). Default pytest run never touches the live APIs.
  • Paid backends. Modal + Daytona cost real money in smoke. Mitigation: paid smoke gates on LYRA_RUN_PAID_SMOKE=1 separately from LYRA_RUN_SMOKE.
  • Voice / vision install size. faster-whisper + opencv-python bring large wheels. Mitigation: keep them strictly opt-in via lyra[voice] / lyra[vision]; document install size in the README's "Optional features" table.
  • Channel-thread mapping bugs. Cross-channel session bleed-over is a real risk in Slack threads + Discord forums. Mitigation: the Gateway keys sessions on (channel, thread_id) from day one and the contract tests cover the multi-thread case explicitly.

docs.title: 8. Definition of done
docs.text:
• All 15 tasks GREEN with no skipped or xfail-with-strict tests (smoke skips remain acceptable when their env var is unset).
  • docs/feature-parity.md §5f and §1.4–§1.6 reflect every v1.9.0 cell flip; the Verification snapshot is bumped to v0.7.
  • CHANGELOG.md v1.9.0 entry lists every new module + every test count + the sandbox-only failure carry-over status.
  • 2026-04-24-v2.0-frontier.md exists and is ready for execution.
  • Combined pytest packages/lyra-core packages/lyra-cli -q run is ~1343 passed, 0 regressions.

docs.title: Wave F detailed plan — v2.0 "Frontier"
docs.text: Detailed task-by-task plan for the wave that turns Lyra from a parity-baseline harness into a frontier research harness. Authored 2026-04-24, immediately after Wave E shipped (1339 tests passing across the repo, only sandbox-only worktree tests failing because git is unavailable in the dev sandbox).

docs.title: How to read this document
docs.text:
This plan mirrors the structure that worked for Wave B/C/D/E:

  • One section per task. Each section opens with the task ID used in the master roadmap (f1…fN) so todo list updates stay aligned.
  • Goal — Files — Tests — Notes. The same four headings every time, so subagents can be dispatched per-task without a per-task briefing rewrite.
  • No git in this wave either. Continuing the no git allow, just implement it lol directive: every task ends at "tests green." A separate operator handles git add / git commit / git push.

The 17 net-new features (§3 of the parity matrix) and 14 frontier ideas (§4) collapse into the 15 task buckets below — some features are merged when they share infrastructure (the meta- harness + arena, the skill router rewrite + auto-optimizer + synthesis, etc.) so each task is a coherent landing.

docs.title: Task f1 — TDD phase state machine
docs.text:
• Goal. First-class, evidence-driven state machine for the agent loop: IDLE → PLAN → RED → GREEN → REFACTOR → SHIP. Strict mode rejects any transition not backed by an artefact (test result, applied diff, commit). Lenient mode warns but proceeds.
  • Files.
  • packages/lyra-core/src/lyra_core/tdd/__init__.py
  • packages/lyra-core/src/lyra_core/tdd/state.py — TDDPhase enum + TDDStateMachine class + TransitionError.
  • packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/interactive/session.py — surface session.tdd_phase; /phase slash command renders + sets the state.
  • Tests. packages/lyra-core/tests/test_tdd_state_machine_contract.py pinning every legal transition, every illegal transition, and the evidence shape required for each (PlanArtifact, RedFailureArtifact, GreenPassArtifact, ShipArtifact).
  • Notes. No external deps. Composes with the safety monitor
  • verifier — TDD-state events flow through the existing HIR emitter so dashboards see them for free.

docs.title: Task f2 — Cross-channel verifier
docs.text:
• Goal. Before any assistant turn surfaces, compare the model trace's claims against ground truth. Three channels are cross-checked:
  • The model's own narration ("I edited foo.py:34").
  • The actual git diff applied this turn.
  • A live filesystem snapshot of the cited file.

Mismatches trigger a verifier.miscite HIR event and (in strict=True) abort the turn before the user reads it. * Files. * packages/lyra-core/src/lyra_core/verifier/cross_channel.py * packages/lyra-core/src/lyra_core/verifier/__init__.py — re-export. * Tests. packages/lyra-core/tests/test_cross_channel_verifier_contract.py with golden cases for: (a) accurate citations pass, (b) wrong line numbers fail, © a non-existent file fails, (d) diff trimmed by whitespace still passes. * Notes. Re-uses the existing unified_diff helper from the replay engine (Wave-E Task 12). When git is missing we degrade gracefully — only the trace-vs-FS check runs.

docs.title: Task f3 — Refute-or-Promote adversarial stage
docs.text:
• Goal. SWE-TRACE-style adversary: a sub-agent attempts to refute the proposed solution. If refute succeeds, control loops back to PLAN with the counter-evidence appended. If it fails to refute after max_attempts, the solution is promoted.
  • Files.
  • packages/lyra-core/src/lyra_core/loop/refute_or_promote.py
  • Wires into the existing review/verification pipeline; no slash command yet (lives behind /review --auto from Task f9).
  • Tests. packages/lyra-core/tests/test_refute_or_promote_contract.py with stub model callables that always-refute / never-refute / refute-once-then-give-up.
  • Notes. The adversary is a regular LLMClient — same surface as the rest of lyra_core, easy to mock. Token budget controlled by the same EFFORT env knob as the main loop.

docs.title: Task f4 — Rubric Process Reward Model
docs.text:
• Goal. Pluggable judge: rubrics under lyra_core/eval/rubrics/<name>.yaml, judge model called per rubric, weighted score returned. Replaces the binary pass/fail-only gate of the Wave-E eval corpus (which stays as the deterministic floor).
  • Files.
  • packages/lyra-core/src/lyra_core/eval/rubrics/__init__.py
  • packages/lyra-core/src/lyra_core/eval/prm.py — Rubric, RubricSet, prm_score.
  • Tests. packages/lyra-core/tests/test_prm_contract.py with a stub judge that returns canned per-rubric scores; pin weighted- average maths and per-rubric drift detection.
  • Notes. Hooked into /eval-drift (Task f9) and the drift gate from Wave-E.

docs.title: Task f5 — Neural Garbage Collection compactor
docs.text:
• Goal. Replace the v1.7.3 LLM-driven /compact for opt-in users. Grow-then-evict context compactor logs every keep/evict decision plus the downstream outcome to compactor-outcomes.jsonl for offline training of a smaller classifier.
  • Files.
  • packages/lyra-core/src/lyra_core/context/ngc.py
  • packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/interactive/session.py — /compact ngc arg.
  • Tests. packages/lyra-core/tests/test_ngc_compactor_contract.py: fake corpus → fake usage signal → assert evicted entries + outcomes JSONL written.
  • Notes. Outcome log shape: {"turn": int, "kept": [...], "evicted": [...], "outcome": "pass"|"fail"}.

docs.title: Task f6 — Reuse-first hybrid skill router
docs.text:
• Goal. Replace the trigger-matcher with BM25 + dense + skill- description match. Explicit verdict tags: MATCH, AMBIGUOUS, NO_MATCH. The router never silently chooses between siblings.
  • Files.
  • packages/lyra-core/src/lyra_core/skills/router.py
  • packages/lyra-core/src/lyra_core/skills/__init__.py — export.
  • Tests. packages/lyra-core/tests/test_skill_router_contract.py with three skills + ten queries pinning each verdict.
  • Notes. BM25 via rank-bm25 (already in scope), dense via the in-process embedder shipped in v1.7.5; vector store stays optional (lyra[vector]).

docs.title: Task f7 — Trigger description auto-optimizer
docs.text:
• Goal. lyra skills tune <name> runs a 5-iteration bounded optimisation loop on a 60/40 eval split, persisting the highest-scoring trigger description back into the skill file.
  • Files.
  • packages/lyra-core/src/lyra_core/skills/tune.py
  • packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/cli.py — skills tune subcmd.
  • Tests. packages/lyra-core/tests/test_skill_tuner_contract.py with a stub LLM that always proposes one specific edit; pin convergence + persistence.
  • Notes. Hard cap on iterations; cost reported per run.

docs.title: Task f8 — In-session skill synthesis
docs.text:
• Goal. Repetition detector + bundled-script detector + /creator slash command + outcome attribution so successful bundled flows graduate to first-class skills.
  • Files.
  • packages/lyra-core/src/lyra_core/skills/synthesis.py
  • packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/interactive/session.py — /creator command.
  • Tests. packages/lyra-core/tests/test_skill_synthesis_contract.py: feed a fake transcript with a 3x repeat, assert the synth proposes a skill stub.
  • Notes. Consumes the replay engine (Wave-E Task 12) for the repetition pass.

docs.title: Task f9 — /review --auto post-turn verifier
docs.text:
• Goal. A single /review --auto that fans out to: TDD-state gate (Task f1), cross-channel verifier (Task f2), safety monitor (Wave-E), refute-or-promote (Task f3), and rubric PRM (Task f4). Renders one consolidated report; --block flag halts the turn on any failure.
  • Files.
  • packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/interactive/session.py — _cmd_review extended.
  • packages/lyra-core/src/lyra_core/loop/auto_review.py — fan-out + merge.
  • Tests. packages/lyra-cli/tests/test_review_auto_contract.py with every stage stubbed in turn.

docs.title: Task f10 — Harness plugins as first-class
docs.text:
• Goal. --harness=dag-teams|single-agent|three-agent|agentless|codeact|meta command-line arg. Each is a registered HarnessPlugin with plan / execute / verify hooks; the meta plugin is Lyra optimising its own harness.
  • Files.
  • packages/lyra-core/src/lyra_core/harness/plugin.py (new HarnessPlugin ABC + registry).
  • packages/lyra-core/src/lyra_core/harness/builtins/ (one file per built-in plugin).
  • packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/cli.py — --harness flag.
  • Tests. packages/lyra-core/tests/test_harness_plugin_contract.py — registry round-trip, default registration, contract assertions on each builtin.
  • Notes. The meta plugin reads from the parity-harness corpus (Task f11) and is itself driven by another plugin during eval.

docs.title: Task f11 — Meta-harness outer loop + parity harness corpus
docs.text:
• Goal. Lyra optimises its own harness against the user's repo via a meta-task that runs the parity-harness corpus (tests/harness-corpus/<task>.yaml). Outputs a per-harness score grid.
  • Files.
  • packages/lyra-core/src/lyra_core/harness/meta.py
  • tests/harness-corpus/ — YAML fixtures for the corpus.
  • Tests. packages/lyra-core/tests/test_meta_harness_contract.py with a 3-task corpus + 2 stub harnesses; pin score-grid maths.

docs.title: Task f12 — Harness arena
docs.text:
• Goal. lyra arena --harnesses dag-teams,three-agent --task "..." runs blind A/B tournaments and produces a publishable winner report (markdown + JSON).
  • Files.
  • packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/cli.py — arena subcmd.
  • packages/lyra-core/src/lyra_core/harness/arena.py.
  • Tests. packages/lyra-core/tests/test_harness_arena_contract.py with two stub harnesses + one synthetic task; pin tie-break logic
  • audit-trail format.
  • Notes. Re-uses the worktree allocator from Wave D.

docs.title: Task f13 — Federated skill registry
docs.text:
• Goal. Sign-then-publish skill packs with sigstore. Pluggable transports (HTTP, S3, IPFS). Policy admission lets an org block specific publishers.
  • Files.
  • packages/lyra-core/src/lyra_core/skills/registry.py — Publisher, Transport, AdmissionPolicy.
  • packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/cli.py — skills publish / skills install subcmds.
  • Tests. packages/lyra-core/tests/test_skill_registry_contract.py with a fake-signing transport (no network); pin admission rejection
  • audit-log shape.
  • Notes. lyra[sigstore] extra; degrades to "audit-log only" when sigstore is missing.

docs.title: Task f14 — KLong checkpoint & resume across model generations
docs.text:
• Goal. Snapshot every state at compaction boundaries (post-NGC) so a new model can resume an old session with the full state context. The snapshot includes plan, evidence ledger, TDD phase, skill cache.
  • Files.
  • packages/lyra-core/src/lyra_core/context/checkpoint.py
  • packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/interactive/session.py — /resume <checkpoint-id>.
  • Tests. packages/lyra-core/tests/test_checkpoint_resume_contract.py with a synthetic snapshot + resume cycle; pin lossless replay of the TDD state machine.

docs.title: Task f15 — Frontier UX bundle: /split+/vote, /observe, ambient budget meter, IDE bridges, plus catch-up grab bag
docs.text:
• Goal. Land the remaining frontier items as a single bundle so Wave F closes cleanly:
  • /split + /vote — fork into N parallel candidates with different models; evaluator picks the winner.
  • /observe live panel — Rich Live region streaming tool calls, token deltas, hook fires.
  • Ambient budget meter — status-bar widget with $ burn rate + >3σ alerts.
  • Skill diff — semantic delta between proposed + existing skill on conflict.
  • Outcome-weighted memory retrieval.
  • Mutation testing pass (mutmut) wired into /review --auto.
  • Policy-as-code debugger.
  • Graph-of-sessions DAG view.
  • Intent embeddings.
  • Failure-first learning hook.
  • /self dogfood gate.
  • VS Code / Zed / JetBrains bridge on the v1.7.2 ACP scaffold.
  • Files. One module per item under packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/frontier/ with explicit registration in the slash registry; IDE bridges live in packages/lyra-ide-bridge/ (new sibling package).
  • Tests. One contract test per item under packages/lyra-cli/tests/frontier/test_<item>_contract.py. Use the standard stub-pattern from Wave-E (no network, no external dependency).
  • Notes. This is intentionally the "long tail" task. The smaller items each fit in <150 LOC; tests stay <60 LOC each. The big lift is the IDE bridge — schedule it last in the wave.

docs.title: Closing the wave
docs.text:
When f1…f15 are merged:

  1. Update master roadmap. Mark Wave F SHIPPED in 2026-04-24-full-parity-roadmap.md. Record the post-merge pytest -q count.
  2. Update parity matrix. Move every ★ row from §3 of feature-parity.md into the "Shipped, parity (Wave F)" section; ditto every "frontier" idea from §4.
  3. CHANGELOG. Add the v2.0.0 "Frontier" entry — bullet per task, link to this plan + the master roadmap.
  4. Wave G plan. Wave G is the integration-debt wave (no new features, only consolidation). Its plan template lives at 2026-04-24-v2.0.x-integration-debt.md and is empty until Wave F closes — same convention as Wave E → Wave F.

docs.title: v2.1 "Claude-Code-Class" Rebuild — Implementation Plan
docs.text:
For agentic workers: Execute task-by-task in this session. Each phase ends with a full-repo regression: pytest -q must show ≥1530 passed / ≤2 skipped / 0 failed. Steps use - [ ] syntax for tracking.

Goal: Ship a production-grade Lyra CLI that lets users pick from any of 6 first-class providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, Qwen, Ollama), paste API keys interactively, and actually run agent tasks end-to-end. Remove mock from every user-facing surface. Adopt UX from claw-code and opencode.

Architecture: In-place refactor with versioned module boundaries. Every phase keeps the test suite green. Default UX changes from day one — no env-var to opt in.

Tech Stack: Python 3.11+, Typer (CLI), prompt_toolkit (REPL), Rich (TTY rendering), harness_core (agent loop), lyra_core (planner/auth/plan), pytest (TDD).

Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-26-production-rebuild-design.md

No-git note: Per the user's directive, do not invoke git add / git commit between tasks. Verification gates are pytest runs.

docs.title: Master roadmap
docs.text:
Phase 1 (v2.1.0) — Foundation:           wire AgentLoop, default to auto, kill mock cascade
Phase 2 (v2.1.1) — Provider Registry:    Qwen first-class, preflight, diagnostics
Phase 3 (v2.1.2) — Connect Flow:         lyra connect, picker dialog, auth.json
Phase 4 (v2.1.3) — Planner Robustness:   tolerant parser, prose fallback, stronger prompt
Phase 5 (v2.1.4) — Status Bar v2:        opencode-style footer + claw-code-style banner
Phase 6 (v2.1.5) — Tool Rendering v2:    per-tool renderers + approval prompt copy
Phase 7 (v2.1.6) — Command Palette v2:   registry-of-records, Ctrl+K, categories
Phase 8 (v2.1.7) — Onboarding:           welcome screen, rotating placeholders

Verification gate after each phase:

cd /Users/kane.nguyendinhquangkhanh/Downloads/Explore/research/harness-engineering/projects/lyra
python3 -m pytest -q
# Expected: ≥1530 passed, ≤2 skipped, 0 failed

docs.title: Phase 1 — Foundation (v2.1.0)
docs.text: Phase goal: After this phase, lyra run --llm deepseek "say hello" makes a real DeepSeek API call and returns the model's response. The REPL no longer shows Model mock when an API key is configured.

docs.title: Task 1.1: Audit existing tests that depend on model="mock" default
docs.text:
Files: - Read: packages/lyra-cli/tests/ (all) - Read: packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/interactive/session.py:151

  • Step 1: Catalogue the call sites by searching model="mock" and Model mock. Confirm count is ~12 places. (Already verified: test_interactive_banner.py × 6, test_slash_blame_trace_self.py × 1, interactive/store.py × 1, plus a few transient InteractiveSession(model="mock", ...) constructions in other tests.)

  • Step 2: Decide policy — for each test, either (a) make model argument explicit (preserve original semantics), or (b) update assertions to expect "auto" post-rebuild. Default to (a) for narrow tests; (b) for tests whose intent is "REPL default behavior".

docs.title: Task 1.2: RED — write failing tests for the new contract
docs.text:
Files: - Create: packages/lyra-cli/tests/test_run_executes_agent_loop.py - Create: packages/lyra-cli/tests/test_repl_default_is_auto.py - Create: packages/lyra-cli/tests/test_no_provider_friendly_error.py

  • Step 1: Write test_run_executes_agent_loop.py
"""Phase 1 — `lyra run` must invoke AgentLoop, not exit early after planning."""
from __future__ import annotations
import os
from pathlib import Path

import pytest
from typer.testing import CliRunner

from lyra_cli.__main__ import app

runner = CliRunner()


@pytest.fixture
def isolated_repo(tmp_path: Path, monkeypatch) -> Path:
    """Empty repo with no `.lyra/` artefacts; fresh slate per test."""
    monkeypatch.chdir(tmp_path)
    monkeypatch.setenv("LYRA_HOME", str(tmp_path / ".lyra-home"))
    return tmp_path


def test_run_no_plan_invokes_agent_loop(isolated_repo: Path, monkeypatch):
    """`lyra run --no-plan --llm mock` must call AgentLoop.run and exit 0
    (mock provider IS legal here since this test is internal)."""
    calls: dict[str, int] = {"plan": 0, "exec": 0}

    def fake_loop_run(self, task, initial_messages=None):
        from harness_core.loop import LoopResult
        calls["exec"] += 1
        return LoopResult(
            completed=True,
            final_message="hello world",
            tool_calls=[],
            tokens_used=10,
            cost_usd=0.0,
        )

    monkeypatch.setattr("harness_core.loop.AgentLoop.run", fake_loop_run)
    result = runner.invoke(
        app,
        ["run", "--no-plan", "--llm", "mock", "say hi"],
        catch_exceptions=False,
    )
    assert calls["exec"] == 1, "AgentLoop.run was never called"
    assert result.exit_code == 0
    assert "hello world" in result.output


def test_run_with_plan_approval_invokes_agent_loop(isolated_repo, monkeypatch):
    """After plan auto-approval, the agent loop must run."""
    exec_calls = {"n": 0}

    def fake_run(self, task, initial_messages=None):
        from harness_core.loop import LoopResult
        exec_calls["n"] += 1
        return LoopResult(
            completed=True, final_message="done",
            tool_calls=[], tokens_used=5, cost_usd=0.0,
        )

    monkeypatch.setattr("harness_core.loop.AgentLoop.run", fake_run)
    result = runner.invoke(
        app,
        ["run", "--auto-approve", "--llm", "mock", "do thing"],
    )
    assert exec_calls["n"] == 1
    assert result.exit_code == 0
  • Step 2: Run the test — expect it to FAIL (red)
cd /Users/kane.nguyendinhquangkhanh/Downloads/Explore/research/harness-engineering/projects/lyra
python3 -m pytest packages/lyra-cli/tests/test_run_executes_agent_loop.py -xvs
# Expected: FAILED — AgentLoop.run is never called because run.py exits early
  • Step 3: Write test_repl_default_is_auto.py
"""Phase 1 — REPL defaults to 'auto' (was 'mock'). Status bar reflects real provider."""
from __future__ import annotations
from pathlib import Path

import pytest

from lyra_cli.interactive.session import InteractiveSession


def test_session_default_model_is_auto(tmp_path: Path):
    s = InteractiveSession(repo_root=tmp_path)
    assert s.model == "auto", f"expected 'auto', got {s.model!r}"


def test_repl_with_anthropic_key_resolves_to_anthropic(monkeypatch, tmp_path):
    monkeypatch.setenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "sk-ant-test-fake")
    monkeypatch.setattr("lyra_cli.llm_factory._anthropic_available", lambda: True)
    from lyra_cli.llm_factory import describe_selection
    label = describe_selection("auto")
    assert label.startswith("anthropic"), f"got {label}"
    assert "mock" not in label
  • Step 4: Write test_no_provider_friendly_error.py
"""Phase 1 — when no provider is reachable, fail with friendly guidance, not mock."""
from __future__ import annotations
from pathlib import Path

import pytest
from typer.testing import CliRunner

from lyra_cli.__main__ import app
from lyra_cli.llm_factory import build_llm

runner = CliRunner()

PROVIDER_KEYS = (
    "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "OPENAI_API_KEY", "GEMINI_API_KEY", "GOOGLE_API_KEY",
    "DEEPSEEK_API_KEY", "XAI_API_KEY", "GROK_API_KEY", "GROQ_API_KEY",
    "CEREBRAS_API_KEY", "MISTRAL_API_KEY", "OPENROUTER_API_KEY",
    "DASHSCOPE_API_KEY",
)


@pytest.fixture
def no_keys(monkeypatch):
    for k in PROVIDER_KEYS:
        monkeypatch.delenv(k, raising=False)
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        "lyra_cli.llm_factory.ollama_reachable", lambda: False
    )
    yield


def test_build_llm_auto_with_no_keys_raises_no_provider(no_keys):
    from lyra_cli.llm_factory import NoProviderConfigured
    with pytest.raises(NoProviderConfigured) as exc_info:
        build_llm("auto")
    assert "lyra connect" in str(exc_info.value)


def test_lyra_run_auto_with_no_keys_exits_2(no_keys, tmp_path, monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.chdir(tmp_path)
    result = runner.invoke(app, ["run", "--llm", "auto", "task"])
    assert result.exit_code == 2
    assert "no provider configured" in result.output.lower()
    assert "lyra connect" in result.output
  • Step 5: Run all three new test files — confirm they all FAIL
python3 -m pytest packages/lyra-cli/tests/test_run_executes_agent_loop.py \
                  packages/lyra-cli/tests/test_repl_default_is_auto.py \
                  packages/lyra-cli/tests/test_no_provider_friendly_error.py -v
# Expected: 6+ failures (none of the new behaviors implemented yet)

docs.title: Task 1.3: GREEN — defaults to "auto" + NoProviderConfigured
docs.text:
Files: - Modify: packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/__main__.py:65-69 - Modify: packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/interactive/session.py:151 - Modify: packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/interactive/store.py:102 - Modify: packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/llm_factory.py (add exception, remove mock cascade fallback)

  • Step 1: Add NoProviderConfigured exception to llm_factory.py

In packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/llm_factory.py, near the top imports:

class NoProviderConfigured(RuntimeError):
    """Raised by build_llm('auto') when no provider is reachable.

    The auto cascade NEVER falls back to mock in v2.1+. Users must
    either set a provider env var or run `lyra connect` to configure
    one in `~/.lyra/auth.json`.
    """

    def __init__(self, message: str | None = None) -> None:
        super().__init__(
            message
            or (
                "no provider configured. Run `lyra connect` to add one "
                "(supports anthropic, openai, gemini, deepseek, qwen, "
                "ollama, and 7 more)."
            )
        )

docs.title: Task 1.4: GREEN — wire harness_core.AgentLoop into run_command
docs.text:
Files: - Modify: packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/commands/run.py - Read: projects/orion-code/harness_core/src/harness_core/loop.py:39-110 (verify AgentLoop ctor and run signature)

  • Step 1: Read loop.py to confirm the contract
sed -n '39,110p' projects/orion-code/harness_core/src/harness_core/loop.py

Expected: a class AgentLoop with fields llm, tools, max_steps, tracer, and run(task: str, initial_messages: Optional[list[Message]] = None) -> LoopResult.

  • Step 2: Add the execution wiring at the end of run_command

In packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/commands/run.py, replace the trailing block:

    _console.print(
        "[cyan]Phase 2 CLI[/cyan] currently stops here; "
        "execution loop ships in Phase 3"
    )

with:

    # Execute the task end-to-end via the harness AgentLoop. Plan Mode
    # was the gate; now we drive the actual model + tools.
    from harness_core.loop import AgentLoop
    from harness_core.tracer import NoopTracer

    if "tools" not in locals():  # --no-plan branch didn't build them
        tools = ToolRegistry()
        register_builtin_tools(tools, repo_root=repo_root)
    if "provider" not in locals():
        provider = build_llm(llm, task_hint=task, session_id=_new_session_id())

    loop = AgentLoop(
        llm=provider,
        tools=tools,
        max_steps=max_steps,
        tracer=NoopTracer(),
    )
    try:
        result = loop.run(task=task)
    except NoProviderConfigured as e:
        _console.print(
            f"[red]{e}[/red]\n\n"
            "→ run [cyan]lyra connect[/cyan] to configure a provider."
        )
        raise typer.Exit(code=2) from e

    if result.final_message:
        _console.print(result.final_message)
    raise typer.Exit(code=0 if result.completed else 1)
  • Step 3: Add max_steps parameter to run_command signature

In the run_command function signature, add:

    max_steps: int = typer.Option(
        25,

"""Phase 2 — `qwen` is a first-class provider name (alias of dashscope)."""
from __future__ import annotations
import pytest

from lyra_cli.llm_factory import known_llm_names
from lyra_cli.providers.openai_compatible import preset_by_name


def test_qwen_in_known_llm_names():
    assert "qwen" in known_llm_names()


def test_qwen_resolves_to_dashscope_preset():
    p = preset_by_name("qwen")
    assert p is not None
    assert p.base_url.startswith("https://dashscope")
    assert "DASHSCOPE_API_KEY" in p.env_keys


def test_qwen_default_model_is_qwen_plus():
    p = preset_by_name("qwen")
    assert p.default_model == "qwen-plus"


def test_dashscope_alias_still_works_for_back_compat():
    p = preset_by_name("dashscope")
    assert p is not None  # legacy callers continue to work
  • Step 2: Write test_preflight_contract.py
"""Phase 2 — preflight performs a cheap auth check before persisting keys."""
from __future__ import annotations
import pytest

from lyra_core.auth.preflight import preflight, PreflightResult


def test_preflight_returns_dataclass(monkeypatch):
    """Mocked HTTP 200 → PreflightResult(ok=True, ...)."""
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        "lyra_core.auth.preflight._http_get",
        lambda url, headers, timeout: (200, b'{"data": [{"id": "model-x"}]}'),
    )
    r = preflight("openai", "sk-fake")
    assert isinstance(r, PreflightResult)
    assert r.ok is True
    assert r.provider == "openai"
    assert r.model_count == 1


def test_preflight_401_returns_bad_key(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        "lyra_core.auth.preflight._http_get",
        lambda url, headers, timeout: (401, b'{"error": "invalid api key"}'),
    )
    r = preflight("openai", "sk-bad")
    assert r.ok is False
    assert "invalid" in r.detail.lower() or "401" in r.detail


def test_preflight_429_returns_rate_limited(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        "lyra_core.auth.preflight._http_get",
        lambda url, headers, timeout: (429, b'{"error": "rate limit"}'),
    )
    r = preflight("openai", "sk-fake")
    assert r.ok is False
    assert "rate" in r.detail.lower() or "429" in r.detail
  • Step 3: Run all four — expect FAIL (no qwen preset, no lyra_core.auth package)

docs.title: v3.3 — Phase M: Token Observatory — Implementation Plan
v3.3 — Phase M: Token Observatory — Implementation Plan
docs.text:
For agentic workers: Execute task-by-task in this session. Each task is TDD (RED → GREEN). Steps use - [ ] syntax for tracking. Per the user's standing directive, do not invoke git add / git commit between tasks — verification gates are pytest runs.

Goal: Ship lyra burn, a first-party cost & activity observatory that answers "where did my AI spend go?" without leaving Lyra. It reads the JSONL transcripts Phase L already writes (<repo>/.lyra/sessions/<id>/turns.jsonl) and renders a Rich TUI with model burn-down, activity classification, one-shot rate, retry detection, optimization hints, model-vs-model comparison, and git-yield correlation.

Architecture: Pure-Python, additive. New package lyra_cli.observatory owns parsing/aggregation/classification/pricing/rendering. New Typer subcommand lyra burn wires it to the CLI. Zero changes to the agent loop or session writers — we consume the existing JSONL contract that Phase L locked in. Pricing fetched via LiteLLM's public catalog (HTTPS, with on-disk cache + hardcoded fallback so airgapped runs still produce sensible numbers). No new third-party deps required (Rich + urllib only).

Tech Stack: Python 3.11+, Rich 13.x (already a Lyra dep), Typer (already wired), urllib.request (stdlib — avoid adding httpx/requests), subprocess for git correlation, pytest for TDD.

Spec / source material: - CodeBurn upstream — https://github.com/getagentseal/codeburn (read in research turn dated 2026-04-27) - Internal research note — uploaded codeburn-0.md (research turn dated 2026-04-27) - Phase L plumbing — packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/interactive/session.py (_TurnSnapshot, _persist_turn, _persist_chat_exchange, JSONL kinds turn and chat)

No-git note: Per the user's directive, do not invoke git add / git commit between tasks. The only place Phase M shells out to git is inside lyra burn yield to read commit history (read-only git log / git show); the implementation itself is committed by the user later.

docs.title: Data contract — what turns.jsonl carries (Phase L, frozen)
docs.text:
Every consumer in this plan reads from <repo>/.lyra/sessions/<sid>/turns.jsonl. Each line is one JSON object. The two relevant kinds:

// kind = "turn" — one user input dispatched to the agent (slash, plain, or chat)
{
  "kind": "turn", "turn": 12, "ts": 1714214410.123,
  "user_input": "fix the failing test in tests/foo.py",
  "command": null,                   // present when slash command, e.g. "rewind"
  "mode": "agent",                   // agent | plan | debug | ask
  "model": "deepseek-v4-pro",        // canonical slug; absent on slash-only turns
  "tokens_in": 1820, "tokens_out": 412,
  "cost_delta_usd": 0.0034,
  "latency_ms": 8421.0
}

// kind = "chat" — the user→assistant text exchange that produced the turn
{
  "kind": "chat", "turn": 12,
  "user": "fix the failing test in tests/foo.py",
  "assistant": "I'll start by running the test...",
  "model": "deepseek-v4-pro",
  "tokens_in": 1820, "tokens_out": 412,
  "cost_delta_usd": 0.0034, "latency_ms": 8421.0,
  "ts": 1714214418.544
}

All optional fields may be absent on rows written before Phase L (v3.1 and earlier). Every reader in this plan must tolerate missing keys and never crash when a transcript is partial. Old → new compat is a hard requirement.

Tool calls are not yet on disk in v3.2 — they live in the in-memory _lifecycle log only. Phase M.1's classifier therefore reads assistant text and user_input (for hints) and treats tool detection as best-effort regex against the assistant text (Edit(, Bash(, Read(, etc.). A future v3.4 task can promote tools to JSONL; the classifier has a documented hook for it (see M.1.4).

docs.title: Task M.1 — Activity classifier (13 categories + retry counter)
docs.text:
Goal: Given any turns.jsonl row, return Classification(category, confidence, was_retry, retry_streak, signals) deterministically. Port CodeBurn's classifier.ts keyword sets verbatim, plus Lyra-specific extensions for our slash commands and our four-mode taxonomy.

Files: - Create: packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/observatory/__init__.py - Create: packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/observatory/classifier.py - Create: packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/observatory/fixtures/__init__.py - Create: packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/observatory/fixtures/sample_transcripts.py - Create: packages/lyra-cli/tests/test_observatory_classifier.py

docs.title: Categories (Literal type)
docs.text:
TaskCategory = Literal[
    "coding", "debugging", "feature", "refactor", "test",
    "explore", "plan", "delegation", "git", "build",
    "brainstorm", "conversation", "general",
]

docs.title: Public surface
docs.text:
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Classification:
    category: TaskCategory
    confidence: float           # 0.0 – 1.0
    was_retry: bool             # True iff prior turn in same session is also coding/debugging
    retry_streak: int           # consecutive same-category turns including this one
    signals: tuple[str, ...]    # human-readable reasons for audit; e.g. "regex:fix", "tool:Edit"


def classify_turn(
    row: Mapping[str, Any],
    *,
    prev: Classification | None = None,
) -> Classification: ...


def one_shot_rate(rows: Iterable[Mapping[str, Any]]) -> float:
    """Fraction of coding turns where retry_streak == 1."""

fixtures/sample_transcripts.py:

"""Tiny JSONL-as-list-of-dicts fixtures used by every Phase M test.

Keeping these as Python literals (not on-disk fixtures) makes the test
suite self-contained and ~5× faster than reading from tmp_path."""
from __future__ import annotations
from typing import Any, Mapping

# A canonical "happy path" 4-turn coding session.
CODING_HAPPY_PATH: list[Mapping[str, Any]] = [
    {"kind": "turn", "turn": 1, "ts": 1.0, "user_input": "add validation",
     "mode": "plan",  "model": "deepseek-v4-flash",
     "tokens_in": 200, "tokens_out": 80,  "cost_delta_usd": 0.0008,
     "latency_ms": 1200.0},
    {"kind": "chat", "turn": 1, "user": "add validation",
     "assistant": "Plan: 1. find input handler 2. add isinstance check...",
     "model": "deepseek-v4-flash"},
    {"kind": "turn", "turn": 2, "ts": 2.0, "user_input": "go",
     "mode": "agent", "model": "deepseek-v4-pro",
     "tokens_in": 1800, "tokens_out": 420, "cost_delta_usd": 0.0042,
     "latency_ms": 6800.0},
    {"kind": "chat", "turn": 2, "user": "go",
     "assistant": "I'll Edit(handlers/api.py) to add the isinstance guard...",
     "model": "deepseek-v4-pro"},
]

# A "fix the bug" debugging session — should classify as debugging not coding.
DEBUGGING_SESSION: list[Mapping[str, Any]] = [
    {"kind": "turn", "turn": 1, "ts": 1.0,
     "user_input": "fix the failing test in tests/foo.py",
     "mode": "debug", "model": "deepseek-v4-pro",
     "tokens_in": 800, "tokens_out": 300, "cost_delta_usd": 0.0021,
     "latency_ms": 4200.0},
    {"kind": "chat", "turn": 1,
     "user": "fix the failing test in tests/foo.py",
     "assistant": "Reading the traceback... AttributeError on line 42.",
     "model": "deepseek-v4-pro"},
]

# Three retry turns in a row — used to test retry_streak.
RETRY_STREAK: list[Mapping[str, Any]] = [
    {"kind": "turn", "turn": 1, "ts": 1.0,
     "user_input": "implement the cache", "mode": "agent",
     "model": "deepseek-v4-pro",
     "tokens_in": 600, "tokens_out": 200, "cost_delta_usd": 0.0015,
     "latency_ms": 3000.0},
    {"kind": "turn", "turn": 2, "ts": 2.0,
     "user_input": "still broken, try again", "mode": "agent",
     "model": "deepseek-v4-pro",
     "tokens_in": 700, "tokens_out": 250, "cost_delta_usd": 0.0018,
     "latency_ms": 3200.0},
    {"kind": "turn", "turn": 3, "ts": 3.0,
     "user_input": "no, that's wrong, fix the cache key collision",
     "mode": "agent", "model": "deepseek-v4-pro",
     "tokens_in": 800, "tokens_out": 280, "cost_delta_usd": 0.0021,
     "latency_ms": 3500.0},
]

# Pure conversation — no code intent.
CONVERSATION: list[Mapping[str, Any]] = [
    {"kind": "turn", "turn": 1, "ts": 1.0,
     "user_input": "hi, what can you help me with today?",
     "mode": "ask", "model": "deepseek-v4-flash",
     "tokens_in": 50, "tokens_out": 120, "cost_delta_usd": 0.0003,
     "latency_ms": 900.0},
    {"kind": "chat", "turn": 1, "user": "hi, what can you help me with today?",
     "assistant": "I'm Lyra. I can help with coding, plans, debugging, "
                  "and answering questions about your repo.",

]
  • Step 2 — Write failing classifier tests (RED)

tests/test_observatory_classifier.py:

"""Phase M.1 — deterministic activity classifier."""
from __future__ import annotations

import pytest

from lyra_cli.observatory.classifier import (
    Classification,
    TaskCategory,
    classify_turn,
    one_shot_rate,
)
from lyra_cli.observatory.fixtures.sample_transcripts import (
    CODING_HAPPY_PATH,
    CONVERSATION,
    DEBUGGING_SESSION,
    RETRY_STREAK,
)


# --- category resolution ---------------------------------------------------

@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "user_input,expected",
    [
        ("fix the failing test in tests/foo.py", "debugging"),
        ("debug why the login route 500s",        "debugging"),
        ("add a settings page",                   "feature"),
        ("implement payment webhook",             "feature"),
        ("refactor the user service",             "refactor"),
        ("rename getCwd to getCurrentWorkingDirectory", "refactor"),
        ("write unit tests for the cart",         "test"),
        ("explain how routing works",             "explore"),
        ("how does the auth middleware work",     "explore"),
        ("plan the migration to postgres",        "plan"),
        ("delegate this to a subagent",           "delegation"),
        ("git status",                            "git"),
        ("commit and push",                       "git"),
        ("npm run build",                         "build"),
        ("brainstorm naming for the new module",  "brainstorm"),
        ("hi, what can you help me with",         "conversation"),
        ("yo",                                    "conversation"),
    ],
)
def test_classify_via_user_input_keywords(user_input: str, expected: TaskCategory):
    row = {"kind": "turn", "turn": 1, "user_input": user_input, "mode": "agent"}
    assert classify_turn(row).category == expected


def test_classify_falls_through_to_general():
    row = {"kind": "turn", "turn": 1,
           "user_input": "asdfghjkl qwerty", "mode": "agent"}
    assert classify_turn(row).category == "general"


# --- tool-based signals override weak keyword matches ----------------------

def test_edit_tool_in_assistant_promotes_to_coding():
    row = {"kind": "chat", "turn": 1,
           "user": "do the thing",
           "assistant": "I'll Edit(src/foo.py) to add the guard."}
    cls = classify_turn(row)
    assert cls.category == "coding"
    assert "tool:Edit" in cls.signals


def test_bash_tool_alone_does_not_promote_to_coding():
    row = {"kind": "chat", "turn": 1, "user": "list files",
           "assistant": "I'll run Bash(ls -la)."}
    assert classify_turn(row).category != "coding"


# --- mode-aware tie-breaks --------------------------------------------------

def test_plan_mode_biases_toward_plan_for_neutral_input():
    row = {"kind": "turn", "user_input": "do the next step", "mode": "plan"}
    assert classify_turn(row).category == "plan"


def test_debug_mode_biases_toward_debugging():
    row = {"kind": "turn", "user_input": "this is broken", "mode": "debug"}
    assert classify_turn(row).category == "debugging"


# --- retry counter & one-shot rate -----------------------------------------

def test_retry_streak_increments_on_same_category():
    prev = None
    streaks: list[int] = []
    for row in RETRY_STREAK:
        cls = classify_turn(row, prev=prev)
        streaks.append(cls.retry_streak)
        prev = cls
    assert streaks == [1, 2, 3]


def test_was_retry_false_on_first_turn():
    cls = classify_turn(RETRY_STREAK[0], prev=None)
    assert cls.was_retry is False
    assert cls.retry_streak == 1


def test_was_retry_true_on_subsequent_same_category():
    first = classify_turn(RETRY_STREAK[0], prev=None)
    second = classify_turn(RETRY_STREAK[1], prev=first)
    assert second.was_retry is True


def test_streak_resets_on_category_change():
    code = {"kind": "turn", "user_input": "implement X", "mode": "agent"}
    convo = {"kind": "turn", "user_input": "thanks!", "mode": "ask"}
    a = classify_turn(code, prev=None)
    b = classify_turn(convo, prev=a)
    assert b.retry_streak == 1
    assert b.was_retry is False


def test_one_shot_rate_two_codings_one_retry():
    rows = [
        {"kind": "turn", "user_input": "implement X", "mode": "agent"},   # 1-shot
        {"kind": "turn", "user_input": "still broken, try again",         # retry
         "mode": "agent"},
        {"kind": "turn", "user_input": "implement Y", "mode": "agent"},   # 1-shot
    ]
    rate = one_shot_rate(rows)
    assert rate == pytest.approx(2 / 3, abs=0.001)


def test_one_shot_rate_zero_codings_returns_one():
    """Convention: vacuous one-shot rate is 1.0 (no failures observed)."""
    assert one_shot_rate(CONVERSATION) == 1.0


# --- robustness -------------------------------------------------------------

def test_missing_user_input_falls_through_to_general():
    assert classify_turn({"kind": "turn"}).category == "general"


def test_chat_kind_with_no_assistant_does_not_crash():
    cls = classify_turn({"kind": "chat", "user": "hi"})
    assert isinstance(cls, Classification)


def test_command_field_classifies_as_general_for_slash_only():
    """Pure slash commands (no LLM) shouldn't pollute coding metrics."""
    row = {"kind": "turn", "command": "rewind", "user_input": "/rewind 1"}
    assert classify_turn(row).category == "general"


# --- confidence -------------------------------------------------------------

def test_strong_signal_yields_high_confidence():
    row = {"kind": "chat", "user": "fix the bug",
           "assistant": "Edit(src/x.py)... fixed the off-by-one."}
    assert classify_turn(row).confidence >= 0.8


def test_weak_keyword_yields_modest_confidence():
    row = {"kind": "turn", "user_input": "thing"}
    assert 0.0 <= classify_turn(row).confidence <= 0.5

``<repo>/.lyra/sessions/<id>/turns.jsonl``. See
``docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-27-v3.3-phase-m-token-observatory.md``
for the design.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/observatory/classifier.py:

"""Deterministic 13-category activity classifier.

Ported from CodeBurn ``classifier.ts``
(https://github.com/getagentseal/codeburn) with Lyra-specific
extensions: our 4-mode taxonomy biases ties, and pure slash-command
turns short-circuit to ``general`` so they don't pollute coding/debug
metrics.

Signal precedence (highest → lowest):
1. Tool name in assistant text (``Edit(``, ``Write(``, ``Read(``…)
2. Strong keyword regex on user_input (``fix bug``, ``add feature``…)
3. Lyra mode (``debug`` → debugging, ``plan`` → plan, etc.)
4. Weak keyword regex
5. Fallback: ``general``

Determinism: every classifier call produces the same result for the
same row + prev. No timestamps, randomness, or external state.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import re
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Any, Iterable, Literal, Mapping

TaskCategory = Literal[
    "coding", "debugging", "feature", "refactor", "test",
    "explore", "plan", "delegation", "git", "build",
    "brainstorm", "conversation", "general",
]


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Classification:
    category: TaskCategory
    confidence: float
    was_retry: bool
    retry_streak: int
    signals: tuple[str, ...]


# ---- regex tables (compiled once) -----------------------------------------

_RX = {
    "debugging": re.compile(
        r"\b(fix|debug|broken|fails?|failing|error|exception|"
        r"trace ?back|crash(?:es|ed)?|stack(?:trace)?|"
        r"why does .* (?:fail|error|crash))\b", re.I),
    "feature":   re.compile(
        r"\b(add|build|implement|create|introduce|ship|new "
        r"(?:feature|page|endpoint|route))\b", re.I),
    "refactor":  re.compile(
        r"\b(refactor|rename|extract|inline|move|split|consolidate|"
        r"deduplicate|clean ?up)\b", re.I),
    "test":      re.compile(
        r"\b(write|add|fix) (?:unit|integration|e2e)? ?tests?\b|"
        r"\btest coverage\b|\bpytest\b", re.I),
    "explore":   re.compile(
        r"\b(explain|how (?:does|do)|where (?:is|does)|what (?:is|does)|"
        r"walk me through|show me)\b", re.I),
    "plan":      re.compile(
        r"\b(plan|design|outline|spec(?:ify)?|approach|strategy)\b", re.I),
    "delegation":re.compile(
        r"\b(delegate|hand ?off|sub ?agent|spawn|fork .* agent)\b", re.I),
    "git":       re.compile(
        r"^(?:/?git\b|commit|push|pull|merge|rebase|branch|stash|cherry-?pick)\b",
        re.I),
    "build":     re.compile(
        r"\b(npm|pnpm|yarn|cargo|make|build|compile|bundle|webpack|"
        r"vite|rollup|tsc)\b", re.I),
    "brainstorm":re.compile(
        r"\b(brainstorm|ideas?|name (?:ideas|suggestions?)|propose)\b", re.I),
    "conversation": re.compile(
        r"^(?:hi|hello|hey|yo|thanks?|thank you|cool|nice|ok(?:ay)?)\b", re.I),
}

_TOOL_RX = re.compile(
    r"\b(Edit|Write|Read|Glob|Grep|Bash|Task|TodoWrite|"
    r"WebFetch|WebSearch)\(", re.I)

_CODING_TOOLS = {"Edit", "Write"}      # produce code-on-disk → coding
_REFACTOR_TOOLS = {"Edit"}             # only when paired with refactor keyword
_TEST_TOOLS = set()                    # promoted via keyword only


# ---- public API ------------------------------------------------------------

def classify_turn(
    row: Mapping[str, Any],
    *,
    prev: Classification | None = None,
) -> Classification:
    signals: list[str] = []

    # Slash-only turns never touch the LLM → never coding/debugging.
    if row.get("command") and not row.get("model"):
        return _finalize("general", 1.0, prev, signals + ["slash-only"])

    user_text = (row.get("user_input") or row.get("user") or "").strip()
    asst_text = (row.get("assistant") or "").strip()
    mode = (row.get("mode") or "").lower()

    # 1) Tool-call detection in assistant.
    tool_hits = _TOOL_RX.findall(asst_text)
    code_tool = any(t in _CODING_TOOLS for t in tool_hits)
    if code_tool:
        signals.append("tool:Edit")
        # If the user input also matches refactor → keep refactor.
        if user_text and _RX["refactor"].search(user_text):
            signals.append("kw:refactor")
            return _finalize("refactor", 0.92, prev, signals)
        if user_text and _RX["test"].search(user_text):
            signals.append("kw:test")
            return _finalize("test", 0.9, prev, signals)
        return _finalize("coding", 0.9, prev, signals)

    # 2) Strong keyword scan on user_input.
    if user_text:
        for cat in (
            "debugging", "test", "refactor", "feature",
            "delegation", "git", "build", "plan",
            "brainstorm", "explore", "conversation",
        ):
            if _RX[cat].search(user_text):
                signals.append(f"kw:{cat}")
                conf = 0.8 if cat in ("debugging", "feature", "test", "refactor") else 0.7
                return _finalize(cat, conf, prev, signals)

    # 3) Mode bias — only when no keyword fired.
    if mode == "debug":
        return _finalize("debugging", 0.5, prev, signals + ["mode:debug"])
    if mode == "plan":
        return _finalize("plan", 0.5, prev, signals + ["mode:plan"])
    if mode == "ask":
        return _finalize("conversation", 0.4, prev, signals + ["mode:ask"])

    # 4) Fallback.
    return _finalize("general", 0.3, prev, signals + ["fallback"])


def one_shot_rate(rows: Iterable[Mapping[str, Any]]) -> float:
    """Fraction of coding/debugging turns where retry_streak == 1.

    Convention: when no coding/debugging turns are present, returns 1.0
    (no failures observed). Callers that want to distinguish "no data"
    from "perfect" should also surface the count.
    """
    prev: Classification | None = None
    n_first_try = 0
    n_total = 0
    for row in rows:
        if row.get("kind") not in (None, "turn"):
            continue  # skip "chat" rows (paired with their turn)
        cls = classify_turn(row, prev=prev)
        if cls.category in ("coding", "debugging"):
            n_total += 1
            if cls.retry_streak == 1:
                n_first_try += 1
        prev = cls
    if n_total == 0:
        return 1.0
    return n_first_try / n_total


# ---- helpers ---------------------------------------------------------------

def _finalize(
    category: TaskCategory, confidence: float,
    prev: Classification | None, signals: list[str],
) -> Classification:
    if prev is not None and prev.category == category:
        streak = prev.retry_streak + 1
        was_retry = True
    else:
        streak = 1
        was_retry = False
    return Classification(
        category=category, confidence=confidence,
        was_retry=was_retry, retry_streak=streak,
        signals=tuple(signals),
    )

docs.title: Task M.2 — LiteLLM pricing engine + on-disk cache
docs.text:
Goal: Resolve cost_per_million(model_slug, kind: "input"|"output") -> Decimal | None from a fresh LiteLLM catalog snapshot, falling back to a hardcoded dict for the top 20 models when offline.

Files: - Create: packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/observatory/pricing.py - Create: packages/lyra-cli/tests/test_observatory_pricing.py

docs.title: Cache layout
docs.text:
~/.cache/lyra/pricing/
    litellm.json          # raw upstream JSON
    litellm.etag          # ETag from upstream (skip download if 304)
    litellm.fetched_at    # ISO8601 string for staleness UI

Cache TTL: 7 days. CLI flag --refresh-pricing (added in M.4) bypasses TTL.

docs.title: Public surface
docs.text:
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PriceQuote:
    model: str
    input_per_mtoken_usd: Decimal | None
    output_per_mtoken_usd: Decimal | None
    source: Literal["upstream", "cache", "fallback", "unknown"]


def quote(model: str, *, refresh: bool = False) -> PriceQuote: ...

def cost_for_turn(row: Mapping[str, Any], *, refresh: bool = False) -> Decimal | None:
    """Recompute cost from tokens × prices, ignoring whatever cost the row
    already carries. Returns None when prices unknown."""

"""Phase M.2 — LiteLLM pricing engine + on-disk cache."""
from __future__ import annotations

import json
from decimal import Decimal
from pathlib import Path

import pytest

from lyra_cli.observatory import pricing


@pytest.fixture
def fake_cache(tmp_path, monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(pricing, "_CACHE_ROOT", tmp_path)
    return tmp_path


def test_quote_uses_fallback_when_no_cache_and_offline(fake_cache, monkeypatch):
    """No network, no cache → must return the hardcoded fallback for known models."""
    monkeypatch.setattr(pricing, "_fetch_upstream", lambda *a, **k: None)
    q = pricing.quote("deepseek-v4-pro")
    assert q.source == "fallback"
    assert q.input_per_mtoken_usd is not None


def test_quote_returns_unknown_for_unknown_model(fake_cache, monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(pricing, "_fetch_upstream", lambda *a, **k: None)
    q = pricing.quote("nonexistent-model-xyz")
    assert q.source == "unknown"
    assert q.input_per_mtoken_usd is None


def test_quote_prefers_cache_over_fallback(fake_cache, monkeypatch):
    cache_payload = {"deepseek-v4-pro": {"input_cost_per_token": 0.000001,
                                          "output_cost_per_token": 0.000004}}
    (fake_cache / "litellm.json").write_text(json.dumps(cache_payload))
    monkeypatch.setattr(pricing, "_fetch_upstream", lambda *a, **k: None)
    q = pricing.quote("deepseek-v4-pro")
    assert q.source == "cache"
    assert q.input_per_mtoken_usd == Decimal("1.0")  # 1e-6 × 1e6
    assert q.output_per_mtoken_usd == Decimal("4.0")


def test_quote_refreshes_on_request(fake_cache, monkeypatch):
    fetched: dict[str, int] = {"n": 0}

    def fake_fetch(*a, **k):
        fetched["n"] += 1
        return {"foo": {"input_cost_per_token": 1e-7,
                        "output_cost_per_token": 1e-7}}

    monkeypatch.setattr(pricing, "_fetch_upstream", fake_fetch)
    pricing.quote("foo", refresh=True)
    pricing.quote("foo", refresh=True)
    assert fetched["n"] == 2  # bypasses TTL


def test_quote_writes_cache_after_successful_fetch(fake_cache, monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        pricing, "_fetch_upstream",
        lambda *a, **k: {"bar": {"input_cost_per_token": 5e-7,
                                 "output_cost_per_token": 5e-7}},
    )
    pricing.quote("bar", refresh=True)
    assert (fake_cache / "litellm.json").exists()


def test_quote_resolves_alias_to_canonical(fake_cache, monkeypatch):
    """`v4-pro` short name should resolve to `deepseek-v4-pro` via Lyra's
    AliasRegistry before pricing lookup."""
    monkeypatch.setattr(pricing, "_fetch_upstream", lambda *a, **k: None)
    q = pricing.quote("v4-pro")
    assert q.model == "deepseek-v4-pro"


def test_cost_for_turn_when_prices_known(fake_cache, monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        pricing, "_fetch_upstream",
        lambda *a, **k: {"deepseek-v4-pro": {
            "input_cost_per_token": 1e-6,
            "output_cost_per_token": 4e-6}},
    )
    row = {"model": "deepseek-v4-pro",
           "tokens_in": 1000, "tokens_out": 500}
    cost = pricing.cost_for_turn(row, refresh=True)
    assert cost == Decimal("0.003000")  # 1000*1e-6 + 500*4e-6 = 0.003


def test_cost_for_turn_returns_none_for_unknown(fake_cache, monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(pricing, "_fetch_upstream", lambda *a, **k: None)
    row = {"model": "nonexistent", "tokens_in": 1000, "tokens_out": 500}
    assert pricing.cost_for_turn(row) is None


def test_ttl_skips_fetch_when_recent(fake_cache, monkeypatch):
    (fake_cache / "litellm.json").write_text('{"x": {"input_cost_per_token": 1e-6, "output_cost_per_token": 1e-6}}')
    (fake_cache / "litellm.fetched_at").write_text("2099-01-01T00:00:00")  # far future = fresh
    fetched = {"n": 0}
    monkeypatch.setattr(pricing, "_fetch_upstream", lambda *a, **k: (fetched.__setitem__("n", fetched["n"]+1) or {}))
    pricing.quote("x")
    assert fetched["n"] == 0
