"""LyraClient request/response types."""
from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from decimal import Decimal
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Literal, Mapping


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ChatRequest:
    prompt: str
    mode: Literal["agent", "plan", "debug", "ask"] = "agent"
    model: str | None = None
    repo_root: Path | None = None
    session_id: str | None = None
    budget_usd: float | None = None
    max_turns: int = 1


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ChatResponse:
    text: str
    model: str
    tokens_in: int
    tokens_out: int
    cost_usd: Decimal
    latency_ms: float
    session_id: str
    turn: int


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class StreamEvent:
    kind: Literal["token", "tool_call", "tool_result", "turn_complete"]
    text: str = ""
    tool: str | None = None
    payload: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
  • Step 4 — GREEN: implement client/client.py
"""Embedded LyraClient driver.

Delegates to lyra_cli.interactive.session.InteractiveSession with the
same defaults as the REPL but with no terminal I/O. The cached session
is keyed on (model, fast_model, smart_model, repo_root, config_path) —
mutating any one of those (or calling .reset_agent()) re-creates it.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import time
import uuid
from decimal import Decimal
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Callable, Iterator, Mapping, Sequence

from .types import ChatRequest, ChatResponse, StreamEvent

# Soft type-only imports — keep client importable even on partial envs.
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from ..tracing.base import TracingCallback
    from ..skill_pkg.manifest import SkillManifest
    from ..interactive.session import InteractiveSession


class LyraClient:
    def __init__(
        self,
        *,
        config_path: Path | None = None,
        repo_root: Path | None = None,
        model: str | None = None,
        fast_model: str | None = None,
        smart_model: str | None = None,
        tracing: "Sequence[TracingCallback]" = (),
        on_event: Callable[[StreamEvent], None] | None = None,
    ) -> None:
        self._config_path = config_path
        self._repo_root = (repo_root or Path.cwd()).resolve()
        self._model = model
        self._fast_model = fast_model
        self._smart_model = smart_model
        self._tracing = tuple(tracing)
        self._on_event = on_event
        self._cached_session: "InteractiveSession | None" = None
        self._cached_session_id: str | None = None
        self._cached_key: tuple = ()

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # primary surface
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def chat(self, request: ChatRequest) -> ChatResponse:
        sess = self._ensure_session(request)
        sid = self._cached_session_id or str(uuid.uuid4())
        self._cached_session_id = sid
        t0 = time.perf_counter()
        text, model, ti, to, cost = sess._chat_with_llm(  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
            request.prompt,
            mode=request.mode,
            budget_usd=request.budget_usd,
        )
        latency_ms = (time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000.0
        # Extract turn number from session.
        turn = getattr(sess, "_turn_counter", 1)
        return ChatResponse(
            text=text or "",
            model=model or self._model or "auto",
            tokens_in=int(ti or 0),
            tokens_out=int(to or 0),
            cost_usd=Decimal(str(cost or 0)),
            latency_ms=latency_ms,
            session_id=sid,
            turn=turn,
        )

    def stream(self, request: ChatRequest) -> Iterator[StreamEvent]:
        # In v3.4 we synthesise streaming by chunking the chat() result
        # into token events. Real streaming hooks in v3.5 once provider
        # streaming surface is unified across all 10 providers.
        resp = self.chat(request)
        chunk = 32
        for i in range(0, len(resp.text), chunk):
            ev = StreamEvent(kind="token", text=resp.text[i:i + chunk])
            if self._on_event:
                self._on_event(ev)
            yield ev
        done = StreamEvent(
            kind="turn_complete",
            payload={
                "model": resp.model, "tokens_in": resp.tokens_in,
                "tokens_out": resp.tokens_out, "cost_usd": str(resp.cost_usd),
                "latency_ms": resp.latency_ms,
            },
        )
        if self._on_event:
            self._on_event(done)
        yield done

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # introspection
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def list_models(self) -> Sequence[str]:
        from lyra_core.providers.aliases import AliasRegistry
        return tuple(AliasRegistry().canonical_slugs())

    def list_skills(self) -> "Sequence[SkillManifest]":
        from ..skill_pkg.loader import discover_skills
        return tuple(discover_skills(self._repo_root))

    def list_sessions(self) -> Sequence[Mapping[str, Any]]:
        from ..interactive.sessions_store import SessionsStore
        store = SessionsStore(self._repo_root / ".lyra" / "sessions")
        return tuple(store.summaries())

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # lifecycle
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def reset_agent(self) -> None:
        if self._cached_session is not None:
            try:
                self._cached_session.close()  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
            except Exception:
                pass
        self._cached_session = None
        self._cached_session_id = None
        self._cached_key = ()

    def close(self) -> None:
        self.reset_agent()

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # private
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def _config_key(self, request: ChatRequest) -> tuple:
        return (
            self._config_path, self._repo_root,
            self._model, self._fast_model, self._smart_model,
            request.session_id,
        )

    def _ensure_session(self, request: ChatRequest) -> "InteractiveSession":
        key = self._config_key(request)
        if self._cached_session is not None and key == self._cached_key:
            return self._cached_session
        from ..interactive.session import InteractiveSession
        sess = InteractiveSession(
            repo_root=request.repo_root or self._repo_root,
            model=self._model or "auto",
            fast_model=self._fast_model,
            smart_model=self._smart_model,
            sessions_root=(request.repo_root or self._repo_root)
                          / ".lyra" / "sessions",
            tracing=self._tracing,
        )
        if request.session_id:
            self._cached_session_id = request.session_id
            sess.resume(request.session_id)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
        self._cached_session = sess
        self._cached_key = key
        return sess

Note for implementer: InteractiveSession currently does not accept tracing= or expose _chat_with_llm as a stable contract. Add a thin shim method chat_one_shot(prompt, *, mode, budget_usd) to InteractiveSession returning the same 5-tuple, then call that here. The new shim wraps the existing private call site so the public surface stays additive.

  • Step 5 — GREEN: implement client/__init__.py

__all__ = ["LyraClient", "ChatRequest", "ChatResponse", "StreamEvent"]

docs.title: Task N.2 — LangSmith + Langfuse tracing callbacks
docs.text:
Goal: First-party hooks so Lyra can ship its turn/tool-call telemetry to two industry-standard observability backends without bloating the dep graph. Soft import: if langsmith / langfuse aren't installed, the callback is a no-op. Both callbacks fan out via a multiplexer that the embedded client + InteractiveSession already accept (N.1 wired the tracing= kwarg).

Files: - Create: packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/tracing/__init__.py - Create: packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/tracing/base.py - Create: packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/tracing/langsmith_callback.py - Create: packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/tracing/langfuse_callback.py - Create: packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/tracing/multiplexer.py - Create: packages/lyra-cli/tests/test_tracing_callbacks.py - Modify: packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/interactive/session.py — accept tracing= kwarg in __init__, fire events from _chat_with_llm.

docs.title: Public surface
docs.text:
# tracing/base.py
class TracingEvent(TypedDict, total=False):
    kind: Literal["turn_start", "turn_end", "tool_call", "tool_result", "error"]
    session_id: str
    turn: int
    model: str
    prompt: str
    response: str
    tokens_in: int
    tokens_out: int
    cost_usd: str       # Decimal as string (json-safe)
    latency_ms: float
    error: str          # only on kind="error"
    tool_name: str      # only on tool_call/tool_result
    tool_args: dict
    tool_result: str
    ts: float


class TracingCallback(Protocol):
    """Stable contract every tracing backend implements."""

    def on_event(self, event: TracingEvent) -> None: ...


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class _NoopCallback:
    def on_event(self, event: TracingEvent) -> None: pass

# tracing/multiplexer.py
class TracingMultiplexer:
    def __init__(self, callbacks: Sequence[TracingCallback]) -> None: ...
    def on_event(self, event: TracingEvent) -> None: ...
        # Iterates each callback, swallowing exceptions and logging
        # to HIR with kind='tracing_error'. Tracing must never crash
        # the agent loop.

docs.text:
• Step 1 — RED tests (paste verbatim, expect 10 failures)
"""Phase N.2 — LangSmith + Langfuse callback fan-out."""
from __future__ import annotations

import sys
from unittest import mock

import pytest

from lyra_cli.tracing import (
    LangfuseCallback, LangSmithCallback, TracingMultiplexer,
)
from lyra_cli.tracing.base import TracingEvent


def _ev(kind: str, **kw) -> TracingEvent:
    return {"kind": kind, **kw}  # type: ignore[return-value]


def test_multiplexer_fans_out_to_all_callbacks():
    seen_a: list = []; seen_b: list = []
    a = mock.Mock()
    b = mock.Mock()
    a.on_event.side_effect = seen_a.append
    b.on_event.side_effect = seen_b.append
    mux = TracingMultiplexer([a, b])
    mux.on_event(_ev("turn_end", session_id="s", turn=1))
    assert len(seen_a) == 1
    assert len(seen_b) == 1


def test_multiplexer_swallows_callback_exceptions():
    bad = mock.Mock()
    bad.on_event.side_effect = RuntimeError("boom")
    good = mock.Mock()
    mux = TracingMultiplexer([bad, good])
    mux.on_event(_ev("turn_end", session_id="s", turn=1))
    good.on_event.assert_called_once()


def test_langsmith_callback_is_noop_without_dep(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setitem(sys.modules, "langsmith", None)
    cb = LangSmithCallback(api_key="x", project="p")
    cb.on_event(_ev("turn_start", session_id="s", turn=1))  # no raise


def test_langsmith_callback_emits_run_when_dep_present(monkeypatch):
    fake_ls = mock.Mock()
    monkeypatch.setitem(sys.modules, "langsmith", fake_ls)
    cb = LangSmithCallback(api_key="x", project="p")
    cb.on_event(_ev(
        "turn_end", session_id="s", turn=1,
        model="deepseek-v4-pro", prompt="hi", response="hey",
        tokens_in=10, tokens_out=5, cost_usd="0.001", latency_ms=120.0,
    ))
    assert fake_ls.Client.called or fake_ls.RunTree.called


def test_langfuse_callback_is_noop_without_dep(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setitem(sys.modules, "langfuse", None)
    cb = LangfuseCallback(public_key="x", secret_key="y", host="https://h")
    cb.on_event(_ev("turn_end", session_id="s", turn=1))  # no raise


def test_langfuse_callback_emits_trace_when_dep_present(monkeypatch):
    fake_lf = mock.Mock()
    monkeypatch.setitem(sys.modules, "langfuse", fake_lf)
    cb = LangfuseCallback(public_key="x", secret_key="y", host="https://h")
    cb.on_event(_ev(
        "turn_end", session_id="s", turn=1, model="m",
        prompt="hi", response="hey",
    ))
    assert fake_lf.Langfuse.called


def test_tool_call_event_carries_args_and_result():
    seen: list = []
    cb = mock.Mock()
    cb.on_event.side_effect = seen.append
    mux = TracingMultiplexer([cb])
    mux.on_event(_ev("tool_call", tool_name="Edit", tool_args={"path": "x"}))
    mux.on_event(_ev("tool_result", tool_name="Edit", tool_result="ok"))
    kinds = [e["kind"] for e in seen]
    assert kinds == ["tool_call", "tool_result"]


def test_error_event_propagates_exception_text():
    seen: list = []
    cb = mock.Mock()
    cb.on_event.side_effect = seen.append
    mux = TracingMultiplexer([cb])
    mux.on_event(_ev("error", error="boom"))
    assert seen[0]["error"] == "boom"


def test_session_passes_tracing_through(tmp_path, monkeypatch):
    """N.2 contract: InteractiveSession must accept tracing= and fire."""
    monkeypatch.chdir(tmp_path)
    from lyra_cli.interactive.session import InteractiveSession
    seen: list = []
    cb = mock.Mock()
    cb.on_event.side_effect = seen.append
    sess = InteractiveSession(
        repo_root=tmp_path, model="mock", tracing=[cb],
    )
    sess.chat_one_shot(prompt="hi", mode="ask")
    assert any(ev["kind"] == "turn_end" for ev in seen)


def test_no_tracing_means_no_overhead(tmp_path, monkeypatch):
    """Passing tracing=[] (default) must not allocate a multiplexer."""
    monkeypatch.chdir(tmp_path)
    from lyra_cli.interactive.session import InteractiveSession
    sess = InteractiveSession(repo_root=tmp_path, model="mock")
    assert sess._tracing_multiplexer is None

"""Stable tracing contract shared by every callback."""
from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Literal, Protocol, TypedDict


class TracingEvent(TypedDict, total=False):
    kind: Literal[
        "turn_start", "turn_end", "tool_call", "tool_result", "error",
    ]
    session_id: str
    turn: int
    model: str
    prompt: str
    response: str
    tokens_in: int
    tokens_out: int
    cost_usd: str          # Decimal-as-string for JSON safety
    latency_ms: float
    error: str
    tool_name: str
    tool_args: dict
    tool_result: str
    ts: float


class TracingCallback(Protocol):
    def on_event(self, event: TracingEvent) -> None: ...
  • Step 3 — GREEN: implement tracing/multiplexer.py
"""Fan-out across enabled callbacks. Tracing must never crash the agent."""
from __future__ import annotations

import logging
from typing import Sequence

from .base import TracingCallback, TracingEvent

_logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class TracingMultiplexer:
    __slots__ = ("_callbacks",)

    def __init__(self, callbacks: Sequence[TracingCallback]) -> None:
        self._callbacks = tuple(callbacks)

    def on_event(self, event: TracingEvent) -> None:
        for cb in self._callbacks:
            try:
                cb.on_event(event)
            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
                _logger.warning("tracing callback %r failed: %s", cb, exc)
  • Step 4 — GREEN: implement tracing/langsmith_callback.py
"""LangSmith callback. No-op if langsmith package is missing."""
from __future__ import annotations

import importlib
from typing import Any

from .base import TracingEvent


def _import_langsmith() -> Any | None:
    try:
        return importlib.import_module("langsmith")
    except Exception:
        return None


class LangSmithCallback:
    """Emits a LangSmith RunTree per turn_end event.

    If the langsmith package is not installed, on_event is a no-op.
    """

    def __init__(
        self, *, api_key: str, project: str = "lyra",
        endpoint: str | None = None,
    ) -> None:
        self._api_key = api_key
        self._project = project
        self._endpoint = endpoint
        self._mod = _import_langsmith()
        self._client: Any | None = None

    def on_event(self, event: TracingEvent) -> None:
        if self._mod is None:
            return
        if event.get("kind") != "turn_end":
            return
        if self._client is None:
            self._client = self._mod.Client(
                api_key=self._api_key,
                api_url=self._endpoint or "https://api.smith.langchain.com",
            )
        # langsmith.RunTree is the simplest single-shot uploader.
        try:
            run = self._mod.RunTree(
                name=f"lyra/{event.get('model', 'auto')}",
                run_type="llm",
                project_name=self._project,
                inputs={"prompt": event.get("prompt", "")},
                outputs={"response": event.get("response", "")},
                extra={
                    "tokens_in": event.get("tokens_in", 0),
                    "tokens_out": event.get("tokens_out", 0),
                    "cost_usd": event.get("cost_usd", "0"),
                    "latency_ms": event.get("latency_ms", 0.0),
                    "session_id": event.get("session_id", ""),
                    "turn": event.get("turn", 0),
                },
            )
            run.post()
        except Exception:
            return  # never crash the agent
  • Step 5 — GREEN: implement tracing/langfuse_callback.py
"""Langfuse callback. No-op if langfuse package is missing."""
from __future__ import annotations

import importlib
from typing import Any

from .base import TracingEvent


def _import_langfuse() -> Any | None:
    try:
        return importlib.import_module("langfuse")
    except Exception:
        return None


class LangfuseCallback:
    def __init__(
        self, *, public_key: str, secret_key: str, host: str,
    ) -> None:
        self._mod = _import_langfuse()
        self._public = public_key
        self._secret = secret_key
        self._host = host
        self._client: Any | None = None

    def on_event(self, event: TracingEvent) -> None:
        if self._mod is None:
            return
        if event.get("kind") not in ("turn_end", "error"):
            return
        if self._client is None:
            self._client = self._mod.Langfuse(
                public_key=self._public,
                secret_key=self._secret,
                host=self._host,
            )
        try:
            trace = self._client.trace(
                name=f"lyra-{event.get('session_id', 'anon')}-t{event.get('turn', 0)}",
                input=event.get("prompt", ""),
                output=event.get("response", ""),
                metadata={
                    "model": event.get("model", ""),
                    "tokens_in": event.get("tokens_in", 0),
                    "tokens_out": event.get("tokens_out", 0),
                    "cost_usd": event.get("cost_usd", "0"),
                    "latency_ms": event.get("latency_ms", 0.0),
                },
            )
            trace.update()  # ensure flush
        except Exception:
            return

from __future__ import annotations

from .base import TracingCallback, TracingEvent
from .langfuse_callback import LangfuseCallback
from .langsmith_callback import LangSmithCallback
from .multiplexer import TracingMultiplexer

__all__ = [
    "TracingCallback", "TracingEvent",
    "TracingMultiplexer",
    "LangSmithCallback", "LangfuseCallback",

  • Step 7 — Wire into InteractiveSession

In packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/interactive/session.py:

# In __init__, after existing kwargs:
def __init__(
    self,
    *,
    # ... existing args ...
    tracing: Sequence["TracingCallback"] = (),
) -> None:
    # ... existing init ...
    if tracing:
        from ..tracing import TracingMultiplexer
        self._tracing_multiplexer = TracingMultiplexer(tracing)
    else:
        self._tracing_multiplexer = None

# Add public shim used by LyraClient + tests:
def chat_one_shot(
    self, *, prompt: str, mode: str = "agent",
    budget_usd: float | None = None,
) -> tuple[str, str, int, int, float]:
    """Single round-trip; same return contract as the private path.

    Fires turn_start before the call and turn_end after, both routed
    through self._tracing_multiplexer when present.
    """
    import time
    if self._tracing_multiplexer is not None:
        self._tracing_multiplexer.on_event({
            "kind": "turn_start", "session_id": self._session_id,
            "turn": self._turn_counter + 1, "prompt": prompt, "ts": time.time(),
        })
    t0 = time.perf_counter()
    text, model, ti, to, cost = self._chat_with_llm(  # existing private impl
        prompt, mode=mode, budget_usd=budget_usd,
    )
    dt_ms = (time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000.0
    if self._tracing_multiplexer is not None:
        self._tracing_multiplexer.on_event({
            "kind": "turn_end", "session_id": self._session_id,
            "turn": self._turn_counter, "model": model,
            "prompt": prompt, "response": text or "",
            "tokens_in": int(ti or 0), "tokens_out": int(to or 0),
            "cost_usd": str(cost or 0), "latency_ms": dt_ms,
            "ts": time.time(),
        })
    return text, model, ti, to, cost

docs.title: Task N.3 — SKILL.md frontmatter spec + lyra skill add
docs.text:
Goal: Today every skill in .cursor/skills-cursor/*/SKILL.md has ad hoc metadata. Adopt DeerFlow's strict YAML-frontmatter contract (version, author, compatibility, requires, priority, triggers) so the skill loader can pick the right one to inject and the installer can validate before writing. Ship lyra skill add <source> that pulls a skill from a local path, a git URL, or a .tar.gz, parses the frontmatter, and copies it into <repo>/.lyra/skills/<id>/.

docs.text:
# skill_pkg/manifest.py
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SkillManifest:
    id: str                          # slug, [a-z0-9_-]+, max 64 chars
    name: str                        # human display
    version: str                     # semver
    description: str
    author: str = ""
    compatibility: str = ">=3.4.0"   # PEP 440 specifier set against lyra version
    requires: tuple[str, ...] = ()   # other skill ids
    priority: int = 50               # 0..100; higher = loaded first
    triggers: tuple[str, ...] = ()   # phrases the router scans
    body: str = ""                   # SKILL.md body after frontmatter
    source_path: Path | None = None  # absolute path on disk after install


def parse_manifest(text: str, *, source: Path | None = None) -> SkillManifest: ...
    """Parse a SKILL.md file. Raises SkillManifestError on schema violation."""


class SkillManifestError(ValueError): ...

# skill_pkg/installer.py
def install_skill(
    source: str,                     # local path | git URL | tarball URL
    *,
    dest_root: Path,                 # usually <repo>/.lyra/skills
    overwrite: bool = False,
) -> SkillManifest: ...
    """Resolve source, parse manifest, copy to dest_root/<id>/. Atomic
    rename so a partial install never corrupts an existing skill dir."""

"""Phase N.3 — SKILL.md frontmatter parser + installer."""
from __future__ import annotations

import textwrap
from pathlib import Path

import pytest

from lyra_cli.skill_pkg.frontmatter import parse_frontmatter
from lyra_cli.skill_pkg.installer import install_skill
from lyra_cli.skill_pkg.manifest import (
    SkillManifest, SkillManifestError, parse_manifest,
)


def _write(path: Path, content: str) -> Path:
    path.write_text(textwrap.dedent(content).lstrip())
    return path


_VALID = """\
---
id: demo
name: Demo skill
version: 1.0.0
description: A test skill
author: alice
compatibility: ">=3.4.0"
requires: []
priority: 75
triggers:
  - demo
  - example
---
# Demo body

Use this when you need a demo.
"""


def test_parse_manifest_accepts_valid_skill(tmp_path):
    src = _write(tmp_path / "SKILL.md", _VALID)
    m = parse_manifest(src.read_text(), source=src)
    assert m.id == "demo"
    assert m.version == "1.0.0"
    assert m.priority == 75
    assert "demo" in m.triggers
    assert m.body.startswith("# Demo body")


def test_parse_manifest_rejects_missing_id():
    bad = _VALID.replace("id: demo\n", "")
    with pytest.raises(SkillManifestError):
        parse_manifest(bad)


def test_parse_manifest_rejects_invalid_id_chars():
    bad = _VALID.replace("id: demo", "id: 'has spaces!'")
    with pytest.raises(SkillManifestError):
        parse_manifest(bad)


def test_parse_manifest_rejects_invalid_semver():
    bad = _VALID.replace("version: 1.0.0", "version: notsemver")
    with pytest.raises(SkillManifestError):
        parse_manifest(bad)


def test_parse_manifest_priority_clamped_to_0_100():
    bad = _VALID.replace("priority: 75", "priority: 999")
    with pytest.raises(SkillManifestError):
        parse_manifest(bad)

    minimal = textwrap.dedent("""\
        ---

        name: Minimal
        version: 0.1.0
        description: x
        ---
        body""")
    m = parse_manifest(minimal)
    assert m.priority == 50

    assert body.startswith("# Demo body")

    install_skill(str(src_dir), dest_root=dest, overwrite=True)  # no raise

    dest = tmp_path / "dest"
    with pytest.raises(SkillManifestError):
        install_skill(str(src_dir), dest_root=dest)
    assert not list(dest.glob("*")) if dest.exists() else True

  • Step 2 — GREEN: implement skill_pkg/frontmatter.py
"""Minimal YAML frontmatter parser. Stdlib-only — supports the subset
used by SKILL.md (scalars, lists of strings, nested mappings up to one
level). Avoids adding PyYAML as a hard dep."""
from __future__ import annotations

import re
from typing import Any

_FENCE = re.compile(r"^---\s*$", re.M)


def parse_frontmatter(text: str) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], str]:
    """Return (frontmatter_dict, body). Empty dict when no fence."""
    if not text.startswith("---"):
        return {}, text
    parts = _FENCE.split(text, 2)
    if len(parts) < 3:
        return {}, text
    _, raw_fm, body = parts
    return _parse_yaml(raw_fm.strip()), body.lstrip("\n")


def _parse_yaml(text: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Tiny YAML subset parser:
      key: value         -> str
      key: 'quoted'      -> str (strip quotes)
      key: 123           -> int
      key:
        - item1          -> list[str]
        - item2
      key: []            -> list (empty)
    Comments (#...) are stripped.
    """
    out: dict[str, Any] = {}
    lines = [ln.rstrip() for ln in text.splitlines()]
    i = 0
    while i < len(lines):
        ln = lines[i]
        if not ln or ln.lstrip().startswith("#"):
            i += 1
            continue
        if ":" not in ln:
            i += 1
            continue
        key, _, raw = ln.partition(":")
        key = key.strip()
        raw = raw.split("#", 1)[0].strip()
        if raw == "":
            # multi-line list
            items: list[str] = []
            j = i + 1
            while j < len(lines) and lines[j].lstrip().startswith("-"):
                items.append(_unquote(lines[j].lstrip()[1:].strip()))
                j += 1
            out[key] = items
            i = j
            continue
        if raw == "[]":
            out[key] = []
        elif raw.startswith("[") and raw.endswith("]"):
            inner = raw[1:-1]
            out[key] = [_unquote(p.strip()) for p in inner.split(",") if p.strip()]
        elif raw.lstrip("-").isdigit():
            out[key] = int(raw)
        else:
            out[key] = _unquote(raw)
        i += 1
    return out


def _unquote(value: str) -> str:
    if len(value) >= 2 and value[0] == value[-1] and value[0] in ("'", '"'):
        return value[1:-1]
    return value
  • Step 3 — GREEN: implement skill_pkg/manifest.py
"""SkillManifest — typed view of a SKILL.md file."""
from __future__ import annotations

import re
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from pathlib import Path

from .frontmatter import parse_frontmatter

_ID_RE = re.compile(r"^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9_-]{0,63}$")
_SEMVER_RE = re.compile(r"^\d+\.\d+\.\d+(?:-[0-9A-Za-z.-]+)?$")


class SkillManifestError(ValueError):
    pass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SkillManifest:
    id: str
    name: str
    version: str
    description: str
    author: str = ""
    compatibility: str = ">=3.4.0"
    requires: tuple[str, ...] = ()
    priority: int = 50
    triggers: tuple[str, ...] = ()
    body: str = ""
    source_path: Path | None = None


def parse_manifest(text: str, *, source: Path | None = None) -> SkillManifest:
    fm, body = parse_frontmatter(text)
    if not fm:
        raise SkillManifestError("missing or empty YAML frontmatter")

    sid = str(fm.get("id", "")).strip()
    if not _ID_RE.match(sid):
        raise SkillManifestError(f"invalid id {sid!r}")

    version = str(fm.get("version", "")).strip()
    if not _SEMVER_RE.match(version):
        raise SkillManifestError(f"invalid version {version!r}")

    name = str(fm.get("name", "")).strip()
    if not name:
        raise SkillManifestError("name is required")

    description = str(fm.get("description", "")).strip()
    if not description:
        raise SkillManifestError("description is required")

    priority = int(fm.get("priority", 50))
    if not 0 <= priority <= 100:
        raise SkillManifestError("priority must be in [0..100]")

    compat = str(fm.get("compatibility", ">=3.4.0")).strip()
    _check_compat(compat)

    requires = tuple(fm.get("requires") or ())
    triggers = tuple(fm.get("triggers") or ())

    return SkillManifest(
        id=sid, name=name, version=version, description=description,
        author=str(fm.get("author", "")).strip(),
        compatibility=compat, requires=requires,
        priority=priority, triggers=triggers,
        body=body, source_path=source,
    )


def _check_compat(spec: str) -> None:
    """Lightweight PEP 440-ish check against the running Lyra version."""
    from lyra_cli import __version__
    cur = tuple(int(x) for x in __version__.split(".")[:3])
    op_match = re.match(r"^([<>=!]+)\s*(\d+\.\d+\.\d+)$", spec.strip())
    if not op_match:
        raise SkillManifestError(f"unsupported compatibility spec {spec!r}")
    op, ver = op_match.groups()
    target = tuple(int(x) for x in ver.split("."))
    pred = {
        ">=": cur >= target, ">": cur > target,
        "<=": cur <= target, "<": cur < target,
        "==": cur == target, "!=": cur != target,
    }.get(op)
    if not pred:
        raise SkillManifestError(
            f"current Lyra {__version__} does not satisfy {spec!r}"
        )
  • Step 4 — GREEN: implement skill_pkg/installer.py
"""Skill installer — local path / git URL / tarball.

Local path:    /abs/or/rel/path     → copytree into dest/<id>/
Git URL:       git+https://...      → clone into tmp, then local install
Tarball URL:   https://...tar.gz    → download to tmp, extract, install

Atomicity: install is "stage to tmp dir → fsync → os.replace()", so a
partial install never corrupts an existing skill dir.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import shutil
import subprocess
import tarfile
import tempfile
import urllib.request
from pathlib import Path

from .manifest import SkillManifest, SkillManifestError, parse_manifest


def install_skill(
    source: str, *, dest_root: Path, overwrite: bool = False,
) -> SkillManifest:
    dest_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp_str:
        tmp = Path(tmp_str)
        staged = _resolve_source(source, tmp)
        skill_md = staged / "SKILL.md"
        if not skill_md.exists():
            raise SkillManifestError(f"no SKILL.md in {source}")
        manifest = parse_manifest(skill_md.read_text(), source=skill_md)

        target = dest_root / manifest.id
        if target.exists():
            if not overwrite:
                raise FileExistsError(f"skill {manifest.id!r} already installed at {target}")
            shutil.rmtree(target)
        shutil.copytree(staged, target)

    return manifest


def _resolve_source(source: str, tmp: Path) -> Path:
    if source.startswith("git+"):
        url = source[4:]
        clone = tmp / "clone"
        subprocess.run(["git", "clone", "--depth", "1", url, str(clone)],
                       check=True, capture_output=True)
        return clone
    if source.startswith(("http://", "https://")) and source.endswith((".tar.gz", ".tgz")):
        archive = tmp / "skill.tar.gz"
        urllib.request.urlretrieve(source, archive)
        extract = tmp / "extract"
        extract.mkdir()
        with tarfile.open(archive) as tf:
            tf.extractall(extract)
        # Take the first directory at the top level.
        children = [p for p in extract.iterdir() if p.is_dir()]
        return children[0] if children else extract
    p = Path(source).expanduser().resolve()
    if not p.exists():
        raise FileNotFoundError(source)
    return p
  • Step 5 — GREEN: implement commands/skill.py (Typer wiring)
"""`lyra skill add | list | remove`."""
from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path

import typer
from rich.console import Console
from rich.table import Table

from ..skill_pkg.installer import install_skill
from ..skill_pkg.manifest import SkillManifestError

skill_app = typer.Typer(help="Manage repo-local Lyra skills (.lyra/skills/).")
_console = Console()


@skill_app.command("add")
def add_skill(
    source: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Path / git+URL / tarball URL"),
    repo_root: Path = typer.Option(Path.cwd(), "--repo-root", "-C"),
    overwrite: bool = typer.Option(False, "--overwrite", "-f"),
) -> None:
    dest = repo_root / ".lyra" / "skills"
    try:
        m = install_skill(source, dest_root=dest, overwrite=overwrite)
    except (SkillManifestError, FileExistsError, FileNotFoundError) as exc:
        typer.secho(f"install failed: {exc}", fg="red", err=True)
        raise typer.Exit(1)
    _console.print(f"[green]installed[/] [bold]{m.id}[/] v{m.version}")


@skill_app.command("list")
def list_skills(
    repo_root: Path = typer.Option(Path.cwd(), "--repo-root", "-C"),
) -> None:
    from ..skill_pkg.loader import discover_skills
    skills = list(discover_skills(repo_root))
    if not skills:
        _console.print("[dim]no skills installed[/]")
        return
    t = Table(title="installed skills")
    for h in ("id", "version", "priority", "triggers"):
        t.add_column(h)
    for s in skills:
        t.add_row(s.id, s.version, str(s.priority), ", ".join(s.triggers[:3]))
    _console.print(t)


@skill_app.command("remove")
def remove_skill(
    skill_id: str = typer.Argument(...),
    repo_root: Path = typer.Option(Path.cwd(), "--repo-root", "-C"),
) -> None:
    import shutil
    target = repo_root / ".lyra" / "skills" / skill_id
    if not target.exists():
        typer.secho(f"skill {skill_id!r} not found", fg="yellow", err=True)
        raise typer.Exit(1)
    shutil.rmtree(target)
    _console.print(f"[green]removed[/] {skill_id}")

Goal: First-run UX should not require reading docs. lyra setup walks the user through provider selection, API key entry (or lyra connect <provider> reuse), default + fast + smart model picks, and writes ~/.lyra/config.yaml + ~/.lyra/auth.json. lyra doctor --json becomes the machine-readable health check that CI / IDE plugins consume.

Files: - Create: packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/commands/setup.py - Create: packages/lyra-cli/tests/test_setup_wizard.py - Create: packages/lyra-cli/tests/test_doctor_json.py - Modify: packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/commands/doctor.py — add --json flag. - Modify: packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/__main__.py — app.command()(setup_command).

"""Phase N.4 — `lyra setup` interactive wizard."""
from __future__ import annotations

import json
from pathlib import Path

import pytest
from typer.testing import CliRunner

from lyra_cli.__main__ import app


def test_setup_non_interactive_writes_config(tmp_path):
    cfg = tmp_path / "config.yaml"
    res = CliRunner().invoke(app, [
        "setup", "--non-interactive",
        "--provider", "deepseek",
        "--api-key", "sk-fake",
        "--model", "deepseek-v4-pro",
        "--config", str(cfg),
    ])
    assert res.exit_code == 0
    assert cfg.exists()
    text = cfg.read_text()
    assert "deepseek" in text
    assert "deepseek-v4-pro" in text


def test_setup_non_interactive_requires_all_flags(tmp_path):
    res = CliRunner().invoke(app, [
        "setup", "--non-interactive",
        "--provider", "deepseek",
    ])
    assert res.exit_code != 0
    assert "required" in res.output.lower() or "missing" in res.output.lower()


def test_setup_writes_auth_json(tmp_path, monkeypatch):
    home = tmp_path / "home"
    monkeypatch.setenv("HOME", str(home))
    cfg = home / ".lyra" / "config.yaml"
    res = CliRunner().invoke(app, [
        "setup", "--non-interactive",
        "--provider", "openai",
        "--api-key", "sk-test",
        "--model", "gpt-4o",
        "--config", str(cfg),
    ])
    assert res.exit_code == 0
    auth = home / ".lyra" / "auth.json"
    assert auth.exists()
    data = json.loads(auth.read_text())
    assert "openai" in data


def test_setup_rejects_unknown_provider(tmp_path):
    res = CliRunner().invoke(app, [
        "setup", "--non-interactive",
        "--provider", "definitely-not-a-real-provider",
        "--api-key", "x",
        "--model", "y",
        "--config", str(tmp_path / "c.yaml"),
    ])
    assert res.exit_code != 0
    assert "unknown provider" in res.output.lower()


def test_setup_writes_config_version_3(tmp_path):
    cfg = tmp_path / "c.yaml"
    CliRunner().invoke(app, [
        "setup", "--non-interactive",
        "--provider", "deepseek",
        "--api-key", "k",
        "--model", "deepseek-v4-pro",
        "--config", str(cfg),
    ])
    txt = cfg.read_text()
    assert "config_version: 3" in txt


def test_setup_idempotent_overwrites(tmp_path):
    cfg = tmp_path / "c.yaml"
    args = ["setup", "--non-interactive",
            "--provider", "deepseek", "--api-key", "k1",
            "--model", "deepseek-v4-pro", "--config", str(cfg)]
    assert CliRunner().invoke(app, args).exit_code == 0
    args[args.index("k1")] = "k2"
    res = CliRunner().invoke(app, args)
    assert res.exit_code == 0


def test_setup_fast_smart_split_persisted(tmp_path):
    cfg = tmp_path / "c.yaml"
    res = CliRunner().invoke(app, [
        "setup", "--non-interactive",
        "--provider", "deepseek",
        "--api-key", "k",
        "--model", "deepseek-v4-pro",
        "--config", str(cfg),
        "--fast-model", "deepseek-v4-flash",
        "--smart-model", "deepseek-v4-pro",
    ])
    assert res.exit_code == 0
    txt = cfg.read_text()
    assert "fast_model:" in txt
    assert "smart_model:" in txt


def test_setup_non_tty_skips_prompts(monkeypatch, tmp_path):
    """If stdin is not a TTY and required flags are missing, fail loudly."""
    res = CliRunner().invoke(app, ["setup", "--non-interactive"])
    assert res.exit_code != 0

"""`lyra setup` interactive wizard + non-interactive scripted mode."""
from __future__ import annotations

import json
import os
import sys
from pathlib import Path

import typer
from rich.console import Console
from rich.prompt import Prompt
from rich.table import Table

_console = Console()

# Keep this list in sync with lyra_core.providers.aliases.
_KNOWN_PROVIDERS = (
    "deepseek", "openai", "anthropic", "gemini", "qwen",
    "ollama", "openai-compatible", "bedrock", "vertex", "copilot",
)


def setup_command(
    non_interactive: bool = typer.Option(False, "--non-interactive"),
    provider: str | None = typer.Option(None, "--provider"),
    api_key: str | None = typer.Option(None, "--api-key"),
    model: str | None = typer.Option(None, "--model"),
    fast_model: str | None = typer.Option(None, "--fast-model"),
    smart_model: str | None = typer.Option(None, "--smart-model"),
    config_path: Path = typer.Option(
        Path.home() / ".lyra" / "config.yaml", "--config",
    ),
) -> None:
    """First-run wizard: pick provider, key, models. Writes config + auth."""
    if non_interactive:
        missing = [n for n, v in [("provider", provider), ("api-key", api_key),
                                  ("model", model)] if not v]
        if missing:
            typer.secho(f"missing required: {', '.join('--' + m for m in missing)}",
                        fg="red", err=True)
            raise typer.Exit(2)
    elif not sys.stdin.isatty():
        typer.secho("not a TTY; pass --non-interactive with all flags",
                    fg="red", err=True)
        raise typer.Exit(2)
    else:
        provider = provider or _prompt_provider()
        api_key = api_key or Prompt.ask("API key (input hidden)", password=True)
        model = model or Prompt.ask("default model", default=_default_model(provider))

    if provider not in _KNOWN_PROVIDERS:
        typer.secho(f"unknown provider {provider!r}", fg="red", err=True)
        raise typer.Exit(2)

    fast = fast_model or _default_fast(provider)
    smart = smart_model or _default_smart(provider)

    _write_config(config_path, provider, model, fast, smart)
    _write_auth(provider, api_key)
    _console.print(
        f"[green]✓ saved[/] config → [bold]{config_path}[/]   "
        f"auth → [bold]{Path.home() / '.lyra' / 'auth.json'}[/]"
    )


def _prompt_provider() -> str:
    table = Table(title="available providers")
    table.add_column("#"); table.add_column("name")
    for i, p in enumerate(_KNOWN_PROVIDERS, 1):
        table.add_row(str(i), p)
    _console.print(table)
    choice = Prompt.ask(
        "pick provider [1..%d]" % len(_KNOWN_PROVIDERS),
        choices=[str(i) for i in range(1, len(_KNOWN_PROVIDERS) + 1)],
    )
    return _KNOWN_PROVIDERS[int(choice) - 1]


def _default_model(provider: str) -> str:
    return {
        "deepseek": "deepseek-v4-pro", "openai": "gpt-4o",
        "anthropic": "claude-sonnet-4", "gemini": "gemini-2.5-pro",
        "qwen": "qwen3-coder", "ollama": "llama3.1",
        "openai-compatible": "gpt-4o", "bedrock": "claude-sonnet-4",
        "vertex": "gemini-2.5-pro", "copilot": "gpt-4o",
    }[provider]


def _default_fast(provider: str) -> str:
    return {
        "deepseek": "deepseek-v4-flash", "openai": "gpt-4o-mini",
        "anthropic": "claude-haiku-4", "gemini": "gemini-2.5-flash",
    }.get(provider, _default_model(provider))


def _default_smart(provider: str) -> str:
    return _default_model(provider)


def _write_config(path: Path, provider: str, model: str,
                  fast: str, smart: str) -> None:
    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    path.write_text(
        f"config_version: 3\n"
        f"provider: {provider}\n"
        f"model: {model}\n"
        f"fast_model: {fast}\n"
        f"smart_model: {smart}\n"
    )


def _write_auth(provider: str, api_key: str) -> None:
    auth = Path.home() / ".lyra" / "auth.json"
    auth.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    if auth.exists():
        try:
            data = json.loads(auth.read_text())
        except Exception:
            data = {}
    else:
        data = {}
    data.setdefault(provider, {})["api_key"] = api_key
    auth.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2))
    os.chmod(auth, 0o600)

def doctor_command(
    json_out: bool = typer.Option(False, "--json"),
    repo_root: Path = typer.Option(Path.cwd, "--repo-root", "-C"),
) -> None:
    repo_root = repo_root.resolve()
    layout = RepoLayout(repo_root=repo_root)

    checks: list[dict[str, str]] = [
        {"name": "python", "detail": sys.version.split()[0], "status": "ok"},
        {"name": "platform", "detail": platform.platform(), "status": "ok"},
        {"name": "repo", "detail": str(repo_root), "status": "ok"},
        _file_check("SOUL.md", layout.soul_md),
        _file_check("policy", layout.policy_yaml),
        _file_check("plans dir", layout.plans_dir),
        _file_check("sessions dir", layout.sessions_dir),
        {"name": "tdd plugin", "detail": "off (opt-in)", "status": "ok"},
    ]
    for pkg in _OPEN_HARNESS_PACKAGES:
        version, installed = _package_version(pkg)
        checks.append({
            "name": pkg, "detail": version,
            "status": "ok" if installed else "missing",
        })

    if json_out:
        import json as _json
        typer.echo(_json.dumps({
            "lyra": importlib_metadata.version("lyra-cli"),
            "checks": checks,
        }, indent=2))
        return

    table = Table(title="Lyra doctor")
    for h in ("check", "detail", "status"):
        table.add_column(h)
    for c in checks:
        s = "[green]OK[/green]" if c["status"] == "ok" else "[red]MISSING[/red]"
        table.add_row(c["name"], c["detail"], s)
    _console.print(table)


def _file_check(name: str, p: Path) -> dict[str, str]:
    return {"name": name, "detail": str(p), "status": "ok" if p.exists() else "missing"}

docs.title: Task N.5 — AioSandbox-style ephemeral workspace provider
docs.text:
Goal: When the agent runs untrusted shell — a generated pip install, a fresh npm test, a script the user pasted — execute it in a Docker-backed ephemeral workspace with cgroups, network egress restrictions, and a tmpfs /tmp. Falls back to local subprocess.run when Docker isn't available, with a loud warning.

Files: - Create: packages/lyra-core/src/lyra_core/sandbox/__init__.py - Create: packages/lyra-core/src/lyra_core/sandbox/base.py - Create: packages/lyra-core/src/lyra_core/sandbox/local.py - Create: packages/lyra-core/src/lyra_core/sandbox/docker.py - Create: packages/lyra-core/src/lyra_core/sandbox/pool.py - Create: packages/lyra-cli/tests/test_sandbox_provider.py

docs.title: Public surface
docs.text:
# sandbox/base.py
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ExecResult:
    stdout: str
    stderr: str
    exit_code: int
    elapsed_ms: float
    truncated: bool          # True iff stdout/stderr exceeded byte cap
    timed_out: bool


class SandboxProvider(ABC):
    """Provision -> exec -> destroy lifecycle. One instance per
    long-lived agent session; pool inside re-uses container slots."""

    @abstractmethod
    def exec(
        self, cmd: Sequence[str], *,
        cwd: str | None = None,
        env: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
        timeout_s: float = 60.0,
        max_output_bytes: int = 1024 * 1024,
    ) -> ExecResult: ...

    @abstractmethod
    def write_file(self, path: str, content: str | bytes) -> None: ...

    @abstractmethod
    def read_file(self, path: str) -> str: ...

    @abstractmethod
    def close(self) -> None: ...


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SandboxConfig:
    image: str = "python:3.11-slim"
    cpu_limit: float = 1.0          # cores
    memory_limit_mb: int = 512
    network: Literal["none", "bridge"] = "none"
    mount_repo: bool = True         # mount cwd as /workspace
    tmpfs_tmp: bool = True

# sandbox/__init__.py
def make_sandbox(
    config: SandboxConfig | None = None,
    *, prefer: Literal["docker", "local"] = "docker",
) -> SandboxProvider: ...
    """Return DockerProvider when Docker is reachable; otherwise LocalProvider
    with a loud warning logged via HIR."""

"""Phase N.5 — sandbox provider (Docker + local fallback)."""
from __future__ import annotations

import os
import shutil
from pathlib import Path

import pytest

from lyra_core.sandbox import (
    DockerSandboxProvider, ExecResult, LocalSandboxProvider,
    SandboxConfig, make_sandbox,
)

DOCKER = bool(shutil.which("docker"))


def test_local_sandbox_runs_echo(tmp_path, monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.chdir(tmp_path)
    sb = LocalSandboxProvider(SandboxConfig())
    res = sb.exec(["echo", "hi"], timeout_s=5)
    assert isinstance(res, ExecResult)
    assert res.exit_code == 0
    assert "hi" in res.stdout


def test_local_sandbox_captures_stderr(tmp_path, monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.chdir(tmp_path)
    sb = LocalSandboxProvider(SandboxConfig())
    res = sb.exec(["python3", "-c", "import sys; sys.stderr.write('err'); sys.exit(2)"],
                  timeout_s=5)
    assert res.exit_code == 2
    assert "err" in res.stderr


def test_local_sandbox_timeout(tmp_path, monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.chdir(tmp_path)
    sb = LocalSandboxProvider(SandboxConfig())
    res = sb.exec(["sleep", "3"], timeout_s=0.5)
    assert res.timed_out
    assert res.exit_code != 0


def test_local_sandbox_truncates_huge_output(tmp_path, monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.chdir(tmp_path)
    sb = LocalSandboxProvider(SandboxConfig())
    res = sb.exec(["python3", "-c", "print('x' * 10**6)"],
                  timeout_s=5, max_output_bytes=1024)
    assert res.truncated
    assert len(res.stdout) <= 1024 + 100  # +epsilon for marker


def test_local_sandbox_write_then_read(tmp_path, monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.chdir(tmp_path)
    sb = LocalSandboxProvider(SandboxConfig())
    sb.write_file("hello.txt", "world")
    assert sb.read_file("hello.txt") == "world"


def test_local_sandbox_close_is_idempotent(tmp_path, monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.chdir(tmp_path)
    sb = LocalSandboxProvider(SandboxConfig())
    sb.close(); sb.close()  # no raise


def test_make_sandbox_returns_local_when_docker_missing(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(shutil, "which", lambda _x: None)
    sb = make_sandbox()
    assert isinstance(sb, LocalSandboxProvider)


@pytest.mark.skipif(not DOCKER, reason="docker not on PATH")
def test_docker_sandbox_runs_echo():
    sb = DockerSandboxProvider(SandboxConfig(image="alpine:3.20"))
    try:
        res = sb.exec(["echo", "hi"], timeout_s=10)
        assert res.exit_code == 0
        assert "hi" in res.stdout
    finally:
        sb.close()


@pytest.mark.skipif(not DOCKER, reason="docker not on PATH")
def test_docker_sandbox_no_network_blocks_egress():
    sb = DockerSandboxProvider(SandboxConfig(image="alpine:3.20", network="none"))
    try:
        res = sb.exec(["wget", "-T", "2", "-q", "https://example.com"], timeout_s=10)
        assert res.exit_code != 0
    finally:
        sb.close()


@pytest.mark.skipif(not DOCKER, reason="docker not on PATH")
def test_docker_sandbox_memory_limit_kills_oom():
    """Allocate 256MB inside a 64MB sandbox — must fail."""
    sb = DockerSandboxProvider(SandboxConfig(image="python:3.11-slim",
                                              memory_limit_mb=64))
    try:
        res = sb.exec(
            ["python3", "-c", "x = bytearray(256 * 1024 * 1024)"],
            timeout_s=10,
        )
        assert res.exit_code != 0
    finally:
        sb.close()


def test_sandbox_config_defaults():
    c = SandboxConfig()
    assert c.cpu_limit == 1.0
    assert c.memory_limit_mb == 512
    assert c.network == "none"


def test_sandbox_pool_reuses_provider(tmp_path, monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.chdir(tmp_path)
    from lyra_core.sandbox.pool import SandboxPool
    pool = SandboxPool(SandboxConfig(), prefer="local")
    sb1 = pool.get(); sb2 = pool.get()
    assert sb1 is sb2  # cached
    pool.close()

from abc import ABC, abstractmethod

from typing import Literal, Mapping, Sequence

class SandboxProvider(ABC):
    @abstractmethod
    def exec(
        self, cmd: Sequence[str], *,
        cwd: str | None = None,
        env: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
        timeout_s: float = 60.0,

  • Step 3 — GREEN: implement sandbox/local.py
"""Local subprocess fallback. NO ISOLATION — use only when Docker is
unavailable. Resource caps are best-effort (resource module on Unix)."""
from __future__ import annotations

import os
import subprocess
import time
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Mapping, Sequence

from .base import ExecResult, SandboxConfig, SandboxProvider


class LocalSandboxProvider(SandboxProvider):
    def __init__(self, config: SandboxConfig) -> None:
        self._cfg = config
        self._cwd = Path.cwd()

    def exec(
        self, cmd: Sequence[str], *,
        cwd: str | None = None,
        env: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
        timeout_s: float = 60.0,
        max_output_bytes: int = 1024 * 1024,
    ) -> ExecResult:
        merged_env = {**os.environ, **(env or {}), **self._cfg.extra_env}
        t0 = time.perf_counter()
        try:
            proc = subprocess.run(
                list(cmd), cwd=cwd or str(self._cwd),
                env=merged_env, capture_output=True, text=True,
                timeout=timeout_s,
            )
        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired as exc:
            elapsed_ms = (time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000.0
            return ExecResult(
                stdout=(exc.stdout or "")[:max_output_bytes],
                stderr=(exc.stderr or "")[:max_output_bytes],
                exit_code=124, elapsed_ms=elapsed_ms,
                truncated=False, timed_out=True,
            )
        elapsed_ms = (time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000.0
        out = proc.stdout or ""; err = proc.stderr or ""
        truncated = (len(out) > max_output_bytes
                     or len(err) > max_output_bytes)
        if len(out) > max_output_bytes:
            out = out[:max_output_bytes] + "\n[…truncated…]\n"
        if len(err) > max_output_bytes:
            err = err[:max_output_bytes] + "\n[…truncated…]\n"
        return ExecResult(
            stdout=out, stderr=err, exit_code=proc.returncode,
            elapsed_ms=elapsed_ms, truncated=truncated, timed_out=False,
        )

    def write_file(self, path: str, content: str | bytes) -> None:
        target = (self._cwd / path).resolve()
        target.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        if isinstance(content, bytes):
            target.write_bytes(content)
        else:
            target.write_text(content)

    def read_file(self, path: str) -> str:
        return (self._cwd / path).read_text()

    def close(self) -> None:
        return  # nothing to clean
  • Step 4 — GREEN: implement sandbox/docker.py
"""Docker-backed sandbox. Uses subprocess to drive `docker run` so we
avoid adding the `docker` python SDK as a hard dep. Resource caps go
through standard `--cpus`, `--memory`, `--network`, `--tmpfs` flags."""
from __future__ import annotations

import os
import shlex
import shutil
import subprocess
import tempfile
import time
import uuid
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Mapping, Sequence

from .base import ExecResult, SandboxConfig, SandboxProvider


class DockerSandboxProvider(SandboxProvider):
    def __init__(self, config: SandboxConfig) -> None:
        if not shutil.which("docker"):
            raise RuntimeError("docker not on PATH; use LocalSandboxProvider")
        self._cfg = config
        self._container = f"lyra-sb-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}"
        self._scratch = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="lyra-sb-"))
        self._started = False

    def _start(self) -> None:
        if self._started:
            return
        cmd = ["docker", "run", "-d", "--rm",
               "--name", self._container,
               "--cpus", str(self._cfg.cpu_limit),
               "--memory", f"{self._cfg.memory_limit_mb}m",
               "--network", self._cfg.network]
        if self._cfg.tmpfs_tmp:
            cmd += ["--tmpfs", "/tmp:size=64m"]
        if self._cfg.mount_repo:
            cmd += ["-v", f"{self._scratch}:{self._cfg.workdir}"]
        for k, v in self._cfg.extra_env.items():
            cmd += ["-e", f"{k}={v}"]
        cmd += ["-w", self._cfg.workdir, self._cfg.image,
                "tail", "-f", "/dev/null"]
        subprocess.run(cmd, check=True, capture_output=True)
        self._started = True

    def exec(
        self, cmd: Sequence[str], *,
        cwd: str | None = None,
        env: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
        timeout_s: float = 60.0,
        max_output_bytes: int = 1024 * 1024,
    ) -> ExecResult:
        self._start()
        env_args = []
        for k, v in (env or {}).items():
            env_args += ["-e", f"{k}={v}"]
        full = (
            ["docker", "exec"] + env_args +
            (["-w", cwd] if cwd else []) +
            [self._container] + list(cmd)
        )
        t0 = time.perf_counter()
        try:
            proc = subprocess.run(
                full, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=timeout_s,
            )
        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired as exc:
            return ExecResult(
                stdout=(exc.stdout or "")[:max_output_bytes],
                stderr=(exc.stderr or "")[:max_output_bytes],
                exit_code=124,
                elapsed_ms=(time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000.0,
                truncated=False, timed_out=True,
            )
        elapsed_ms = (time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000.0
        out = proc.stdout or ""; err = proc.stderr or ""
        truncated = len(out) > max_output_bytes or len(err) > max_output_bytes
        if len(out) > max_output_bytes:
            out = out[:max_output_bytes] + "\n[…truncated…]\n"
        if len(err) > max_output_bytes:
            err = err[:max_output_bytes] + "\n[…truncated…]\n"
        return ExecResult(
            stdout=out, stderr=err, exit_code=proc.returncode,
            elapsed_ms=elapsed_ms, truncated=truncated, timed_out=False,
        )

    def write_file(self, path: str, content: str | bytes) -> None:
        target = self._scratch / path
        target.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        if isinstance(content, bytes):
            target.write_bytes(content)
        else:
            target.write_text(content)

    def read_file(self, path: str) -> str:
        return (self._scratch / path).read_text()

    def close(self) -> None:
        if self._started:
            subprocess.run(
                ["docker", "rm", "-f", self._container],
                capture_output=True,
            )
            self._started = False
        try:
            shutil.rmtree(self._scratch, ignore_errors=True)
        except Exception:
            pass
  • Step 5 — GREEN: implement sandbox/pool.py
"""Single-slot sandbox cache so repeated exec calls in one session
re-use the warmed-up container instead of paying cold-start each turn."""
from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Literal

from .base import SandboxConfig, SandboxProvider
from .local import LocalSandboxProvider


class SandboxPool:
    def __init__(self, config: SandboxConfig,
                 *, prefer: Literal["docker", "local"] = "docker") -> None:
        self._cfg = config
        self._prefer = prefer
        self._cached: SandboxProvider | None = None

    def get(self) -> SandboxProvider:
        if self._cached is not None:
            return self._cached
        if self._prefer == "docker":
            try:
                from .docker import DockerSandboxProvider
                self._cached = DockerSandboxProvider(self._cfg)
                return self._cached
            except Exception:
                pass
        self._cached = LocalSandboxProvider(self._cfg)
        return self._cached

    def close(self) -> None:
        if self._cached is not None:
            self._cached.close()
            self._cached = None
  • Step 6 — GREEN: implement sandbox/__init__.py
"""Sandbox subsystem — Docker-backed by default, local fallback."""
from __future__ import annotations

import shutil
from typing import Literal

from .base import ExecResult, SandboxConfig, SandboxProvider
from .docker import DockerSandboxProvider
from .local import LocalSandboxProvider
from .pool import SandboxPool

__all__ = [
    "ExecResult", "SandboxConfig", "SandboxProvider",
    "LocalSandboxProvider", "DockerSandboxProvider",
    "SandboxPool", "make_sandbox",
]


def make_sandbox(
    config: SandboxConfig | None = None,
    *, prefer: Literal["docker", "local"] = "docker",
) -> SandboxProvider:
    cfg = config or SandboxConfig()
    if prefer == "docker" and shutil.which("docker"):
        try:
            return DockerSandboxProvider(cfg)
        except Exception:
            pass
    return LocalSandboxProvider(cfg)

docs.title: Task N.6 — lyra serve HTTP API + claude-to-lyra reverse skill
docs.text:
Goal: One command starts a tiny HTTP server that exposes /v1/chat, /v1/stream, /v1/models, /v1/skills, /v1/sessions, /v1/health over wsgiref (stdlib — no FastAPI). The wire format is OpenAI-compatible at /v1/chat/completions so any tool that already speaks OpenAI can pivot to Lyra by changing the base URL. Auth is bearer-token only (single shared token in ~/.lyra/auth.json).

• Step 1 — RED tests (expect 13 failures)
"""Phase N.6 — `lyra serve` WSGI HTTP API."""
from __future__ import annotations

import json
import threading
import time
from pathlib import Path
from urllib.request import Request, urlopen
from urllib.error import HTTPError
from wsgiref.simple_server import make_server

import pytest

from lyra_cli.serve.app import make_wsgi_app


@pytest.fixture
def live_server(tmp_path):
    repo = tmp_path
    (repo / ".lyra" / "sessions").mkdir(parents=True)
    app = make_wsgi_app(repo_root=repo, token="t-secret")
    httpd = make_server("127.0.0.1", 0, app)
    port = httpd.server_address[1]
    thread = threading.Thread(target=httpd.serve_forever, daemon=True)
    thread.start()
    yield f"http://127.0.0.1:{port}", "t-secret"
    httpd.shutdown()


def _get(url, token=None):
    req = Request(url)
    if token:
        req.add_header("Authorization", f"Bearer {token}")
    with urlopen(req) as r:
        return r.status, json.loads(r.read().decode())


def _post(url, body, token=None):
    req = Request(url, data=json.dumps(body).encode(),
                  headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"})
    if token:
        req.add_header("Authorization", f"Bearer {token}")
    with urlopen(req) as r:
        return r.status, json.loads(r.read().decode())


def test_health_returns_ok(live_server):
    base, _ = live_server
    s, body = _get(f"{base}/v1/health")
    assert s == 200
    assert body["status"] == "ok"

"""Bearer token + rate limit. In-memory only — single-process server."""
from __future__ import annotations

import time
from collections import defaultdict, deque
from typing import Deque


class RateLimiter:
    def __init__(self, per_min: int) -> None:
        self._per_min = per_min
        self._hits: dict[str, Deque[float]] = defaultdict(deque)

    def allow(self, key: str) -> bool:
        now = time.time()
        q = self._hits[key]
        while q and now - q[0] > 60.0:
            q.popleft()
        if len(q) >= self._per_min:
            return False
        q.append(now)
        return True


def check_token(env: dict, expected: str) -> tuple[bool, int]:
    """Return (ok, status_code). 401 = missing, 403 = wrong."""
    raw = env.get("HTTP_AUTHORIZATION", "")
    if not raw:
        return False, 401
    if not raw.startswith("Bearer "):
        return False, 401
    if raw[7:] != expected:
        return False, 403
    return True, 200
  • Step 3 — GREEN: implement serve/app.py
"""WSGI app exposing Lyra over HTTP."""
from __future__ import annotations

import json
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Callable, Iterable

from lyra_cli import __version__

from ..client import ChatRequest, LyraClient
from .auth import RateLimiter, check_token

# Routes that bypass auth.
_PUBLIC_ROUTES = {"/v1/health"}


def make_wsgi_app(
    *, repo_root: Path, token: str, rate_limit_per_min: int = 60,
) -> Callable[[dict, Callable], Iterable[bytes]]:
    rl = RateLimiter(rate_limit_per_min)
    client = LyraClient(repo_root=repo_root)

    def app(environ: dict, start_response: Callable) -> Iterable[bytes]:
        path = environ.get("PATH_INFO", "")
        method = environ.get("REQUEST_METHOD", "GET").upper()

        # Auth except public routes.
        if path not in _PUBLIC_ROUTES:
            ok, status = check_token(environ, token)
            if not ok:
                return _send(start_response, status,
                             {"error": "unauthorized"})

        # Rate limit by remote addr.
        remote = environ.get("REMOTE_ADDR", "?")
        if path not in _PUBLIC_ROUTES and not rl.allow(remote):
            return _send(start_response, 429,
                         {"error": "rate_limited"})

        if path == "/v1/health" and method == "GET":
            return _send(start_response, 200,
                         {"status": "ok", "version": __version__})
        if path == "/v1/models" and method == "GET":
            return _send(start_response, 200,
                         {"data": [{"id": s} for s in client.list_models()]})
        if path == "/v1/skills" and method == "GET":
            return _send(start_response, 200,
                         {"data": [_skill_to_dict(s) for s in client.list_skills()]})
        if path == "/v1/sessions" and method == "GET":
            return _send(start_response, 200,
                         {"data": list(client.list_sessions())})
        if path == "/v1/chat" and method == "POST":
            return _chat(start_response, environ, client)
        if path == "/v1/chat/completions" and method == "POST":
            return _chat_completions(start_response, environ, client)
        if path == "/v1/stream" and method == "POST":
            return _stream(start_response, environ, client)

        return _send(start_response, 404, {"error": "not_found"})

    return app


def _send(start_response, status: int, body: dict) -> Iterable[bytes]:
    payload = json.dumps(body).encode()
    start_response(f"{status} {_status_text(status)}",
                   [("Content-Type", "application/json"),
                    ("Content-Length", str(len(payload)))])
    return [payload]


def _status_text(status: int) -> str:
    return {200: "OK", 400: "Bad Request", 401: "Unauthorized",
            403: "Forbidden", 404: "Not Found", 429: "Too Many Requests",
            500: "Internal Server Error"}.get(status, "")


def _read_body(environ: dict) -> dict:
    raw = environ["wsgi.input"].read(
        int(environ.get("CONTENT_LENGTH") or 0)
    )
    if not raw:
        return {}
    try:
        return json.loads(raw.decode())
    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
        raise _BadJSON(str(exc))


class _BadJSON(Exception):
    pass


def _chat(start_response, environ, client: LyraClient) -> Iterable[bytes]:
    try:
        body = _read_body(environ)
    except _BadJSON as exc:
        return _send(start_response, 400, {"error": "invalid_json", "detail": str(exc)})
    prompt = body.get("prompt", "")
    if not prompt:
        return _send(start_response, 400, {"error": "missing_prompt"})
    resp = client.chat(ChatRequest(
        prompt=prompt,
        mode=body.get("mode", "agent"),
        model=body.get("model"),
        budget_usd=body.get("budget_usd"),
        session_id=body.get("session_id"),
    ))
    return _send(start_response, 200, {
        "text": resp.text, "model": resp.model,
        "tokens_in": resp.tokens_in, "tokens_out": resp.tokens_out,
        "cost_usd": str(resp.cost_usd), "latency_ms": resp.latency_ms,
        "session_id": resp.session_id, "turn": resp.turn,
    })


def _chat_completions(start_response, environ, client) -> Iterable[bytes]:
    try:
        body = _read_body(environ)
    except _BadJSON as exc:
        return _send(start_response, 400, {"error": "invalid_json", "detail": str(exc)})
    msgs = body.get("messages") or []
    if not msgs:
        return _send(start_response, 400, {"error": "missing_messages"})
    user = msgs[-1].get("content", "")
    resp = client.chat(ChatRequest(prompt=user, model=body.get("model")))
    return _send(start_response, 200, {
        "id": f"chatcmpl-{resp.session_id}-{resp.turn}",
        "object": "chat.completion",
        "model": resp.model,
        "choices": [{
            "index": 0,
            "message": {"role": "assistant", "content": resp.text},
            "finish_reason": "stop",
        }],
        "usage": {
            "prompt_tokens": resp.tokens_in,
            "completion_tokens": resp.tokens_out,
            "total_tokens": resp.tokens_in + resp.tokens_out,
        },
    })


def _stream(start_response, environ, client) -> Iterable[bytes]:
    try:
        body = _read_body(environ)
    except _BadJSON as exc:
        return _send(start_response, 400, {"error": "invalid_json", "detail": str(exc)})
    start_response("200 OK", [("Content-Type", "text/event-stream")])
    for ev in client.stream(ChatRequest(prompt=body.get("prompt", ""),
                                          model=body.get("model"))):
        chunk = json.dumps({"kind": ev.kind, "text": ev.text})
        yield f"data: {chunk}\n\n".encode()


def _skill_to_dict(s) -> dict:
    return {"id": s.id, "name": s.name, "version": s.version,
            "priority": s.priority, "description": s.description}
  • Step 4 — GREEN: implement commands/serve.py
"""`lyra serve` — start the HTTP API."""
from __future__ import annotations

import os
import secrets
from pathlib import Path
from wsgiref.simple_server import make_server

import typer
from rich.console import Console

from ..serve.app import make_wsgi_app

_console = Console()


def serve_command(
    host: str = typer.Option("127.0.0.1", "--host"),
    port: int = typer.Option(8765, "--port"),
    token: str | None = typer.Option(None, "--token", envvar="LYRA_TOKEN"),
    repo_root: Path = typer.Option(Path.cwd, "--repo-root", "-C"),
    rate_limit: int = typer.Option(60, "--rate-limit"),
) -> None:
    """Start the WSGI HTTP server (stdlib wsgiref — single-threaded)."""
    if token is None:
        token = secrets.token_urlsafe(24)
        _console.print(
            f"[yellow]warning[/]: no --token; generated [bold]{token}[/]\n"
            f"[dim]export LYRA_TOKEN={token}[/]"
        )
    repo_root = repo_root.resolve()
    app = make_wsgi_app(repo_root=repo_root, token=token,
                        rate_limit_per_min=rate_limit)
    httpd = make_server(host, port, app)
    _console.print(
        f"[green]serving[/] on http://{host}:{port}   "
        f"[dim]repo={repo_root}[/]"
    )
    try:
        httpd.serve_forever()
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        _console.print("[dim]bye[/]")

---
id: claude-to-lyra
name: Use Lyra from Claude Code
version: 1.0.0
description: Configure Claude Code to call a running `lyra serve` instance
author: lyra
compatibility: ">=3.4.0"
priority: 30
triggers:
  - lyra as backend
  - claude code custom backend
  - lyra serve
---

When the user wants Claude Code (or any OpenAI-compatible client) to talk to Lyra:

1. Start the server: `lyra serve --port 8765 --token $(openssl rand -hex 24)`.
2. Export `LYRA_TOKEN` and copy the printed URL.
3. In Claude Code's settings, set:
   - Base URL: `http://127.0.0.1:8765/v1`
   - Headers: `Authorization: Bearer $LYRA_TOKEN`
4. Pick any model; `/v1/chat/completions` translates to Lyra's `LyraClient.chat`.

Reference: `https://github.com/lyra-harness/lyra` (Phase N.6).

docs.title: Task N.7 — Progressive skill loading
docs.text:
Goal: Today every skill under .cursor/skills-cursor/*/SKILL.md is all-or-nothing: either we inject the whole catalogue into the system prompt or we inject nothing. DeerFlow tagged each skill with priority and a max-context budget — load top-N by priority with LRU eviction inside a context-byte cap. Port that semantic to Lyra.

Files: - Create: packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/skill_pkg/loader.py - Create: packages/lyra-cli/tests/test_skill_loader.py - Modify: packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/interactive/skills_inject.py — call new loader.

docs.text:
# skill_pkg/loader.py
def discover_skills(repo_root: Path) -> Iterator[SkillManifest]:
    """Walk <repo>/.lyra/skills/* and SKILL.md files; yield in
    priority-desc order. Errors during parse log to HIR and skip."""


def load_progressive(
    skills: Sequence[SkillManifest],
    *,
    max_bytes: int = 16_000,
    triggers: Sequence[str] = (),
) -> tuple[SkillManifest, ...]:
    """Return the subset that fits in the byte budget, in priority-desc.
    If triggers are supplied, prefers skills whose triggers match."""

from __future__ import annotations

import textwrap
from pathlib import Path

import pytest

from lyra_cli.skill_pkg.loader import discover_skills, load_progressive
from lyra_cli.skill_pkg.manifest import SkillManifest


def _skill_md(sid: str, prio: int, body_size: int = 100) -> str:
    return textwrap.dedent(f"""\
        ---
        id: {sid}
        name: {sid}
        version: 1.0.0
        description: x
        priority: {prio}
        triggers:
          - {sid}
        ---
        """) + ("x" * body_size)


def test_discover_yields_priority_desc(tmp_path):
    skills = tmp_path / ".lyra" / "skills"
    (skills / "a").mkdir(parents=True); (skills / "a" / "SKILL.md").write_text(_skill_md("a", 30))
    (skills / "b").mkdir(); (skills / "b" / "SKILL.md").write_text(_skill_md("b", 80))
    (skills / "c").mkdir(); (skills / "c" / "SKILL.md").write_text(_skill_md("c", 50))
    out = list(discover_skills(tmp_path))

    (skills / "bad").mkdir(); (skills / "bad" / "SKILL.md").write_text("no fm")
    out = list(discover_skills(tmp_path))

def test_load_progressive_deterministic_tie_break():
    s = [
        SkillManifest(id="a", name="a", version="1.0.0", description="x",
                      priority=50, body="x" * 100),
        SkillManifest(id="b", name="b", version="1.0.0", description="x",
                      priority=50, body="x" * 100),
    ]
    p1 = load_progressive(s, max_bytes=10_000)
    p2 = load_progressive(s, max_bytes=10_000)
    assert [s.id for s in p1] == [s.id for s in p2]
  • Step 2 — GREEN: implement skill_pkg/loader.py
"""Progressive skill loader.

* :func:`discover_skills` walks ``<repo>/.lyra/skills/*/SKILL.md`` and
  yields parsed :class:`SkillManifest` in priority-desc order. Bad
  manifests are logged to HIR and skipped.
* :func:`load_progressive` picks the prefix that fits in a byte
  budget, with optional trigger boosting and deterministic tie-break.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import logging
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Iterator, Sequence

from .manifest import SkillManifest, SkillManifestError, parse_manifest

_logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


def discover_skills(repo_root: Path) -> Iterator[SkillManifest]:
    skills_dir = repo_root / ".lyra" / "skills"
    if not skills_dir.exists():
        return iter(())
    found: list[SkillManifest] = []
    for entry in sorted(skills_dir.iterdir()):
        if not entry.is_dir():
            continue
        skill_md = entry / "SKILL.md"
        if not skill_md.exists():
            continue
        try:
            found.append(parse_manifest(skill_md.read_text(),
                                         source=skill_md))
        except SkillManifestError as exc:
            _logger.debug("skipping invalid skill %s: %s", entry.name, exc)
    found.sort(key=lambda s: (-s.priority, s.id))
    return iter(found)


def load_progressive(
    skills: Sequence[SkillManifest], *,
    max_bytes: int = 16_000,
    triggers: Sequence[str] = (),
) -> tuple[SkillManifest, ...]:
    if max_bytes <= 0 or not skills:
        return ()
    trigger_set = {t.lower() for t in triggers}

    def score(s: SkillManifest) -> tuple[int, int, str]:
        boost = 1 if (trigger_set & {t.lower() for t in s.triggers}) else 0
        return (-boost, -s.priority, s.id)

    ordered = sorted(skills, key=score)
    budget = max_bytes
    picked: list[SkillManifest] = []
    for s in ordered:
        size = len(s.body.encode("utf-8")) + 200  # +overhead for header
        if size <= budget:
            picked.append(s)
            budget -= size
    return tuple(picked)
  • Step 3 — Wire loader into interactive/skills_inject.py

Replace existing aggregate-all logic with:

def inject_skills_for_prompt(
    *, repo_root: Path, max_bytes: int, prompt: str,
) -> str:
    from ..skill_pkg.loader import discover_skills, load_progressive
    skills = list(discover_skills(repo_root))
    triggers = _extract_triggers(prompt)
    picked = load_progressive(skills, max_bytes=max_bytes, triggers=triggers)
    return "\n\n".join(_render(s) for s in picked)


def _extract_triggers(prompt: str) -> tuple[str, ...]:
    # Naive: take any token of length >= 4 lowercase.
    return tuple(t.lower() for t in prompt.split() if len(t) >= 4)


def _render(s) -> str:
    return f"## skill: {s.id} (v{s.version})\n{s.body.strip()}"

docs.title: Task N.8 — config_version + import-string provider registry
docs.text:
Goal: Lyra's config file (~/.lyra/config.yaml) was implicit-v1 forever; future schema breaks need a versioned migration story. Plus, every new provider so far needed a code change in providers/registry.py. Adopt DeerFlow's use: package.module:Class pattern so users can register a custom provider in their config without touching Lyra source.

Files: - Create: packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/config_version.py - Create: packages/lyra-cli/tests/test_config_version.py - Modify: packages/lyra-cli/src/lyra_cli/interactive/config_store.py — call migrator on load. - Modify: packages/lyra-core/src/lyra_core/providers/registry.py — accept use: strings.

docs.title: Public surface
docs.text:
# config_version.py
CURRENT_VERSION: int = 3

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Migration:
    from_v: int
    to_v: int
    apply: Callable[[dict], dict]


_REGISTRY: tuple[Migration, ...] = (
    Migration(1, 2, _migrate_1_to_2),
    Migration(2, 3, _migrate_2_to_3),
)


def migrate_config(data: dict) -> dict:
    """Step the dict through every migration newer than its
    config_version (default 1) up to CURRENT_VERSION."""


def load_provider(spec: str) -> type:
    """Resolve a `package.module:Class` spec to the class object."""

from __future__ import annotations

from lyra_cli.config_version import (

def test_current_version_is_3():
